src 

 1from .app.container import AppContainer
 2from .collections.collection import Collection
 3from .collections.manager import CollectionManager
 4from .configs.app_config import AppConfig, AppPaths
 5from .data.preprocessing import TextPreprocessor
 6from .indexers.base import BaseIndexer
 7from .indexers.bm25_indexer import BM25Indexer
 8from .indexers.word2vec_indexer import Word2VecIndexer, W2VWrapper
 9from .indexers.fasttext_indexer import FastTextIndexer
10from .search.engine import SearchEngine
11from .search.factory import SearchEngineFactory
12from .services.index_build_service import IndexBuildService, IndexBuildReport
13from .services.recommendation_service import RecommendationService
14from .services.search_service import SearchService
15

@dataclass
class AppConfig: 
50@dataclass
51class AppConfig:
52    """Main runtime config for app container"""
53
54    paths: AppPaths
55
56    # corpus columns
57    original_text_column: str = "abstract"
58    processed_text_column: str = "index_text_lemmatised"
59    seed_text_column: str = "index_text_lemmatised"
60
61    # startup behavior
62    ensure_indexes_on_start: bool = True
63    rebuild_indexes_on_start: bool = False
64    indexes_to_ensure: tuple[str, ...] = ("bm25", "word2vec", "fasttext")
65
66    # recommendation defaults
67    recommendation_index_name: str = "fasttext"
68    recommendation_rrf_k: int = 60

Main runtime config for app container

AppConfig( paths: AppPaths, original_text_column: str = 'abstract', processed_text_column: str = 'index_text_lemmatised', seed_text_column: str = 'index_text_lemmatised', ensure_indexes_on_start: bool = True, rebuild_indexes_on_start: bool = False, indexes_to_ensure: tuple[str, ...] = ('bm25', 'word2vec', 'fasttext'), recommendation_index_name: str = 'fasttext', recommendation_rrf_k: int = 60)
paths: AppPaths
original_text_column: str = 'abstract'
processed_text_column: str = 'index_text_lemmatised'
seed_text_column: str = 'index_text_lemmatised'
ensure_indexes_on_start: bool = True
rebuild_indexes_on_start: bool = False
indexes_to_ensure: tuple[str, ...] = ('bm25', 'word2vec', 'fasttext')
recommendation_index_name: str = 'fasttext'
recommendation_rrf_k: int = 60
@dataclass
class AppPaths: 
 8@dataclass
 9class AppPaths:
10    """
11    Unified project paths relative to root directory
12
13    Default paths:
14    - source code: root/src
15    - indexes: root/indexes
16    - models: root/models
17    - csv data: root/csv
18    """
19
20    root: Path
21    src_dir: Path = field(init=False)
22    indexes_dir: Path = field(init=False)
23    models_dir: Path = field(init=False)
24    csv_dir: Path = field(init=False)
25    collections_dir: Path = field(init=False)
26
27    # default model files
28    word2vec_model_path: Path = field(init=False)
29    fasttext_model_path: Path = field(init=False)
30
31    # default corpus files
32    corpus_preprocessed_csv_path: Path = field(init=False)
33    corpus_raw_csv_path: Path = field(init=False)
34
35    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
36        self.root = Path(self.root).resolve()
37        self.src_dir = self.root / "src"
38        self.indexes_dir = self.root / "indexes"
39        self.collections_dir = self.root / "collections"
40        self.models_dir = self.root / "models"
41        self.csv_dir = self.root / "csv"
42
43        self.word2vec_model_path = self.models_dir / "wiki-news-300d-1M.vec"
44        self.fasttext_model_path = self.models_dir / "fasttext_model.bin"
45
46        self.corpus_preprocessed_csv_path = self.csv_dir / "abstracts_lemmatized.csv"
47        self.corpus_raw_csv_path = self.csv_dir / "abstracts.csv"

Unified project paths relative to root directory

Default paths:

  • source code: root/src
  • indexes: root/indexes
  • models: root/models
  • csv data: root/csv
AppPaths(root: pathlib.Path)
root: pathlib.Path
src_dir: pathlib.Path
indexes_dir: pathlib.Path
models_dir: pathlib.Path
csv_dir: pathlib.Path
collections_dir: pathlib.Path
word2vec_model_path: pathlib.Path
fasttext_model_path: pathlib.Path
corpus_preprocessed_csv_path: pathlib.Path
corpus_raw_csv_path: pathlib.Path
class AppContainer: 
 14class AppContainer:
 15    """Application composition root for services and managers"""
 16
 17    def __init__(
 18        self,
 19        config: AppConfig,
 20        preprocessor: Optional[Any] = None,
 21        models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 22        docs_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None,
 23    ) -> None:
 24        """
 25        :param config: app config with paths and startup settings
 26        :param preprocessor: query preprocessor instance
 27        :param models: optional preloaded models dict
 28        :param docs_df: optional preloaded corpus dataframe
 29        """
 30        self.config = config
 31        self.paths = config.paths
 32
 33        self._ensure_base_directories()
 34
 35        # init basic dependencies
 36        # define preprocessor if not given user's one
 37        self.preprocessor = preprocessor or TextPreprocessor()
 38
 39        # define docs dataframe if user's given
 40        self.docs_df = docs_df if docs_df is not None else self._load_docs_df()
 41
 42        # load basic models from config if not models
 43        self.models = models or self._load_models()
 44
 45        # init collection manager with persistence in root/collections
 46        self.collection_manager = CollectionManager(
 47            root_path=self.paths.root,
 48            collections_dir_name=self.paths.collections_dir.name,
 49        )
 50
 51        # init index builder
 52        self.index_build_service = IndexBuildService(
 53            corpus_csv_path=self.paths.corpus_preprocessed_csv_path,
 54            indexes_root=self.paths.indexes_dir,
 55            processed_text_column=self.config.processed_text_column,
 56            models=self.models,
 57        )
 58
 59        # ensure that indexes are created
 60        if self.config.ensure_indexes_on_start:
 61            self.index_build_service.ensure_indexes(
 62                index_names=list(self.config.indexes_to_ensure),
 63                rebuild=self.config.rebuild_indexes_on_start,
 64            )
 65
 66        # init search factory
 67        self.search_engine_factory = SearchEngineFactory(
 68            indexes_root=self.paths.indexes_dir,
 69            preprocessor=self.preprocessor,
 70            original_texts=self.docs_df[self.config.original_text_column].fillna("").astype(str).tolist(),
 71            processed_texts=self.docs_df[self.config.processed_text_column].fillna("").astype(str).tolist(),
 72            models=self.models,
 73        )
 74
 75        # init search service
 76        self.search_service = SearchService(
 77            engine_factory=self.search_engine_factory,
 78            docs_df=self.docs_df,
 79            original_text_column=self.config.original_text_column,
 80            processed_text_column=self.config.processed_text_column,
 81        )
 82
 83        # init recommendation service
 84        self.recommendation_service = RecommendationService(
 85            collection_manager=self.collection_manager,
 86            search_service=self.search_service,
 87            docs_df=self.docs_df,
 88            default_index_name=self.config.recommendation_index_name,
 89            seed_text_column=self.config.seed_text_column,
 90            rrf_k=self.config.recommendation_rrf_k,
 91        )
 92
 93    @classmethod
 94    def from_root(
 95        cls,
 96        root_path: Path,
 97        preprocessor: Optional[Any] = None,
 98        models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 99        docs_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None,
100        **config_kwargs: Any,
101    ) -> "AppContainer":
102        """
103        Build container from root directory and optional config overrides
104        :param root_path: path to project root
105        :param preprocessor: optional preprocessor object
106        :param models: optional preloaded models
107        :param docs_df: optional preloaded dataframe
108        :param config_kwargs: optional AppConfig overrides
109        :returns: app container instance
110        """
111        paths = AppPaths(root=Path(root_path))
112        config = AppConfig(paths=paths, **config_kwargs)
113        return cls(config=config, preprocessor=preprocessor, models=models, docs_df=docs_df)
114
115    def search(
116        self,
117        query: str,
118        index_name: str,
119        top_k: int = 10,
120        filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
121    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
122        """
123        Facade method for search operations
124        :param query: query text
125        :param index_name: bm25, word2vec or fasttext
126        :param top_k: max results
127        :param filters: optional metadata filters
128        :returns: search response
129        """
130        return self.search_service.search_with_filters(
131            query=query,
132            index_name=index_name,
133            top_k=top_k,
134            filters=filters,
135        )
136
137    def create_collection(
138        self,
139        title: str,
140        description: str = "",
141        keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None,
142    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
143        """
144        Facade method to create collection
145        :param title: collection title
146        :param description: collection description
147        :param keywords: optional keywords
148        :returns: serialised collection
149        """
150        collection = self.collection_manager.create_collection(
151            title=title,
152            description=description,
153            keywords=keywords,
154            autosave=True,
155        )
156        return collection.to_dict()
157
158    def add_paper_to_collection(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str) -> bool:
159        """
160        Facade method to add paper to collection.
161        :param collection_id: collection id
162        :param paper_id: stable corpus paper id
163        :returns: true if added
164        """
165        return self.collection_manager.add_paper(collection_id=collection_id, paper_id=paper_id, autosave=True)
166
167    def recommend(
168        self,
169        collection_id: str,
170        top_k: int = 10,
171        filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
172    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
173        """
174        Facade method for recommendation service call
175        :param collection_id: collection id
176        :param top_k: max recommendations
177        :param filters: optional metadata filters
178        :returns: recommendation response
179        """
180        return self.recommendation_service.recommend(
181            collection_id=collection_id,
182            top_k=top_k,
183            filters=filters,
184        )
185
186    def _ensure_base_directories(self) -> None:
187        """
188        Internal function to ensure the default directories from appconfig
189        :return: None
190        """
191        self.paths.indexes_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
192        self.paths.collections_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
193        self.paths.models_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
194        self.paths.csv_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
195
196    def _load_docs_df(self) -> pd.DataFrame:
197        """
198        Load dataframe with corpus metadata and text.
199
200        :returns: dataframe
201        """
202        path = self.paths.corpus_preprocessed_csv_path
203        if not path.exists():
204            # fallback for current project layout
205            fallback = self.paths.src_dir / "data" / "abstracts_lemmatized.csv"
206            if fallback.exists():
207                path = fallback
208            else:
209                raise FileNotFoundError(
210                    f"preprocessed csv not found. checked: {self.paths.corpus_preprocessed_csv_path} and {fallback}"
211                )
212
213        df = pd.read_csv(path).reset_index(drop=True)
214        if self.config.original_text_column not in df.columns:
215            raise ValueError(f'missing column "{self.config.original_text_column}" in corpus csv')
216        if self.config.processed_text_column not in df.columns:
217            raise ValueError(f'missing column "{self.config.processed_text_column}" in corpus csv')
218        return df
219
220    def _load_models(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
221        """
222        Load word2vec and fasttext models if files are present.
223
224        Model loading is best-effort:
225        - word2vec from .vec via KeyedVectors + W2VWrapper
226        - fasttext from facebook .bin via gensim loader
227
228        :returns: models dict
229        """
230        models: dict[str, Any] = {}
231
232        # load word2vec vectors
233        w2v_path = self.paths.word2vec_model_path
234        if not w2v_path.exists():
235            fallback_w2v = self.paths.src_dir / "data" / "wiki-news-300d-1M.vec"
236            w2v_path = fallback_w2v if fallback_w2v.exists() else w2v_path
237
238        if w2v_path.exists():
239            from gensim.models import KeyedVectors
240
241            kv = KeyedVectors.load_word2vec_format(str(w2v_path), binary=False)
242            models["word2vec"] = W2VWrapper(kv)
243
244        # load fasttext model
245        ft_path = self.paths.fasttext_model_path
246        if not ft_path.exists():
247            fallback_ft = self.paths.src_dir / "data" / "model.bin"
248            ft_path = fallback_ft if fallback_ft.exists() else ft_path
249
250        if ft_path.exists():
251            from gensim.models.fasttext import load_facebook_model
252
253            models["fasttext"] = load_facebook_model(str(ft_path))
254
255        return models

Application composition root for services and managers

AppContainer( config: AppConfig, preprocessor: Optional[Any] = None, models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None, docs_df: Optional[pandas.core.frame.DataFrame] = None) 
17    def __init__(
18        self,
19        config: AppConfig,
20        preprocessor: Optional[Any] = None,
21        models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
22        docs_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None,
23    ) -> None:
24        """
25        :param config: app config with paths and startup settings
26        :param preprocessor: query preprocessor instance
27        :param models: optional preloaded models dict
28        :param docs_df: optional preloaded corpus dataframe
29        """
30        self.config = config
31        self.paths = config.paths
32
33        self._ensure_base_directories()
34
35        # init basic dependencies
36        # define preprocessor if not given user's one
37        self.preprocessor = preprocessor or TextPreprocessor()
38
39        # define docs dataframe if user's given
40        self.docs_df = docs_df if docs_df is not None else self._load_docs_df()
41
42        # load basic models from config if not models
43        self.models = models or self._load_models()
44
45        # init collection manager with persistence in root/collections
46        self.collection_manager = CollectionManager(
47            root_path=self.paths.root,
48            collections_dir_name=self.paths.collections_dir.name,
49        )
50
51        # init index builder
52        self.index_build_service = IndexBuildService(
53            corpus_csv_path=self.paths.corpus_preprocessed_csv_path,
54            indexes_root=self.paths.indexes_dir,
55            processed_text_column=self.config.processed_text_column,
56            models=self.models,
57        )
58
59        # ensure that indexes are created
60        if self.config.ensure_indexes_on_start:
61            self.index_build_service.ensure_indexes(
62                index_names=list(self.config.indexes_to_ensure),
63                rebuild=self.config.rebuild_indexes_on_start,
64            )
65
66        # init search factory
67        self.search_engine_factory = SearchEngineFactory(
68            indexes_root=self.paths.indexes_dir,
69            preprocessor=self.preprocessor,
70            original_texts=self.docs_df[self.config.original_text_column].fillna("").astype(str).tolist(),
71            processed_texts=self.docs_df[self.config.processed_text_column].fillna("").astype(str).tolist(),
72            models=self.models,
73        )
74
75        # init search service
76        self.search_service = SearchService(
77            engine_factory=self.search_engine_factory,
78            docs_df=self.docs_df,
79            original_text_column=self.config.original_text_column,
80            processed_text_column=self.config.processed_text_column,
81        )
82
83        # init recommendation service
84        self.recommendation_service = RecommendationService(
85            collection_manager=self.collection_manager,
86            search_service=self.search_service,
87            docs_df=self.docs_df,
88            default_index_name=self.config.recommendation_index_name,
89            seed_text_column=self.config.seed_text_column,
90            rrf_k=self.config.recommendation_rrf_k,
91        )
Parameters
  • config: app config with paths and startup settings
  • preprocessor: query preprocessor instance
  • models: optional preloaded models dict
  • docs_df: optional preloaded corpus dataframe

@classmethod
def from_root( cls, root_path: pathlib.Path, preprocessor: Optional[Any] = None, models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None, docs_df: Optional[pandas.core.frame.DataFrame] = None, **config_kwargs: Any) -> AppContainer: 
 93    @classmethod
 94    def from_root(
 95        cls,
 96        root_path: Path,
 97        preprocessor: Optional[Any] = None,
 98        models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 99        docs_df: Optional[pd.DataFrame] = None,
100        **config_kwargs: Any,
101    ) -> "AppContainer":
102        """
103        Build container from root directory and optional config overrides
104        :param root_path: path to project root
105        :param preprocessor: optional preprocessor object
106        :param models: optional preloaded models
107        :param docs_df: optional preloaded dataframe
108        :param config_kwargs: optional AppConfig overrides
109        :returns: app container instance
110        """
111        paths = AppPaths(root=Path(root_path))
112        config = AppConfig(paths=paths, **config_kwargs)
113        return cls(config=config, preprocessor=preprocessor, models=models, docs_df=docs_df)

def create_collection( self, title: str, description: str = '', keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
137    def create_collection(
138        self,
139        title: str,
140        description: str = "",
141        keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None,
142    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
143        """
144        Facade method to create collection
145        :param title: collection title
146        :param description: collection description
147        :param keywords: optional keywords
148        :returns: serialised collection
149        """
150        collection = self.collection_manager.create_collection(
151            title=title,
152            description=description,
153            keywords=keywords,
154            autosave=True,
155        )
156        return collection.to_dict()

def add_paper_to_collection(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str) -> bool: 
158    def add_paper_to_collection(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str) -> bool:
159        """
160        Facade method to add paper to collection.
161        :param collection_id: collection id
162        :param paper_id: stable corpus paper id
163        :returns: true if added
164        """
165        return self.collection_manager.add_paper(collection_id=collection_id, paper_id=paper_id, autosave=True)

Facade method to add paper to collection.

Parameters
  • collection_id: collection id
  • paper_id: stable corpus paper id :returns: true if added
def recommend( self, collection_id: str, top_k: int = 10, filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
167    def recommend(
168        self,
169        collection_id: str,
170        top_k: int = 10,
171        filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
172    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
173        """
174        Facade method for recommendation service call
175        :param collection_id: collection id
176        :param top_k: max recommendations
177        :param filters: optional metadata filters
178        :returns: recommendation response
179        """
180        return self.recommendation_service.recommend(
181            collection_id=collection_id,
182            top_k=top_k,
183            filters=filters,
184        )

Facade method for recommendation service call

Parameters
  • collection_id: collection id
  • top_k: max recommendations
  • filters: optional metadata filters :returns: recommendation response
@dataclass
class Collection: 
 13@dataclass
 14class Collection:
 15    """Data object for one user collection"""
 16
 17    id: str
 18    title: str
 19    description: str = ""
 20    keywords: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 21    added_papers: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
 22    created_at: str = field(default_factory=_utc_now_iso)
 23    updated_at: str = field(default_factory=_utc_now_iso)
 24
 25    def add_paper(self, paper_id: str) -> bool:
 26        """
 27        Add paper id if it does not exist
 28        :param paper_id: paper id from corpus
 29        :returns: true if paper was added
 30        """
 31        normalised_id = str(paper_id).strip()
 32        if not normalised_id:
 33            return False
 34        if normalised_id in self.added_papers:
 35            return False
 36        self.added_papers.append(normalised_id)
 37        self._touch()
 38        return True
 39
 40    def remove_paper(self, paper_id: str) -> bool:
 41        """
 42        Remove paper id if it exists
 43        :param paper_id: paper id from corpus
 44        :returns: true if paper was removed
 45        """
 46        normalized_id = str(paper_id).strip()
 47        if normalized_id not in self.added_papers:
 48            return False
 49        self.added_papers.remove(normalized_id)
 50        self._touch()
 51        return True
 52
 53    def add_keyword(self, keyword: str) -> bool:
 54        """
 55        Add keyword if it does not exist
 56        :param keyword: keyword text
 57        :returns: true if keyword was added
 58        """
 59        normalized_keyword = str(keyword).strip()
 60        if not normalized_keyword:
 61            return False
 62        if normalized_keyword in self.keywords:
 63            return False
 64        self.keywords.append(normalized_keyword)
 65        self._touch()
 66        return True
 67
 68    def remove_keyword(self, keyword: str) -> bool:
 69        """
 70        Remove keyword if it exists
 71        :param keyword: keyword text
 72        :returns: true if keyword was removed
 73        """
 74        normalized_keyword = str(keyword).strip()
 75        if normalized_keyword not in self.keywords:
 76            return False
 77        self.keywords.remove(normalized_keyword)
 78        self._touch()
 79        return True
 80
 81    def change_title(self, new_title: str) -> bool:
 82        """
 83        Change title if value is not empty and changed
 84        :param new_title: new title text
 85        :returns: true if changed
 86        """
 87        value = str(new_title).strip()
 88        if not value:
 89            return False
 90        if value == self.title:
 91            return False
 92        self.title = value
 93        self._touch()
 94        return True
 95
 96    def change_description(self, new_description: str) -> bool:
 97        """
 98        Change description
 99        :param new_description: new description text
100        :returns: true if changed
101        """
102        value = str(new_description).strip()
103        if value == self.description:
104            return False
105        self.description = value
106        self._touch()
107        return True
108
109    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
110        """
111        Convert collection object to dict
112        :returns: serializable dict
113        """
114        return {
115            "id": self.id,
116            "title": self.title,
117            "description": self.description,
118            "keywords": list(self.keywords),
119            "added_papers": list(self.added_papers),
120            "created_at": self.created_at,
121            "updated_at": self.updated_at,
122        }
123
124    @classmethod
125    def from_dict(cls, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> "Collection":
126        """
127        Build collection from dict
128        :param payload: dict payload
129        :returns: collection object
130        """
131        collection_id = str(payload.get("id", "")).strip()
132        title = str(payload.get("title", "")).strip()
133        if not collection_id:
134            raise ValueError("collection id is required")
135        if not title:
136            raise ValueError("collection title is required")
137
138        keywords = [str(k).strip() for k in payload.get("keywords", []) if str(k).strip()]
139        added_papers = [str(p).strip() for p in payload.get("added_papers", []) if str(p).strip()]
140
141        return cls(
142            id=collection_id,
143            title=title,
144            description=str(payload.get("description", "")).strip(),
145            keywords=keywords,
146            added_papers=added_papers,
147            created_at=str(payload.get("created_at", _utc_now_iso())),
148            updated_at=str(payload.get("updated_at", _utc_now_iso())),
149        )
150
151    def _touch(self) -> None:
152        """Update timestamp after collection mutation"""
153        self.updated_at = _utc_now_iso()

Data object for one user collection

Collection( id: str, title: str, description: str = '', keywords: list[str] = <factory>, added_papers: list[str] = <factory>, created_at: str = <factory>, updated_at: str = <factory>)
id: str
title: str
description: str = ''
keywords: list[str]
added_papers: list[str]
created_at: str
updated_at: str
def add_paper(self, paper_id: str) -> bool: 
25    def add_paper(self, paper_id: str) -> bool:
26        """
27        Add paper id if it does not exist
28        :param paper_id: paper id from corpus
29        :returns: true if paper was added
30        """
31        normalised_id = str(paper_id).strip()
32        if not normalised_id:
33            return False
34        if normalised_id in self.added_papers:
35            return False
36        self.added_papers.append(normalised_id)
37        self._touch()
38        return True

42        Remove paper id if it exists

44        :returns: true if paper was removed
45        """
46        normalized_id = str(paper_id).strip()
47        if normalized_id not in self.added_papers:
48            return False

50        self._touch()

69        """
70        Remove keyword if it exists
71        :param keyword: keyword text
72        :returns: true if keyword was removed
73        """

82        """
83        Change title if value is not empty and changed
84        :param new_title: new title text
85        :returns: true if changed

87        value = str(new_title).strip()
88        if not value:

 97        """
 98        Change description
 99        :param new_description: new description text
100        :returns: true if changed
101        """
102        value = str(new_description).strip()
103        if value == self.description:
104            return False
105        self.description = value

def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
109    def to_dict(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
110        """
111        Convert collection object to dict
112        :returns: serializable dict
113        """
114        return {
115            "id": self.id,
116            "title": self.title,

Convert collection object to dict :returns: serializable dict

@classmethod
def from_dict( cls, payload: dict[str, typing.Any]) -> Collection: 
124    @classmethod
125    def from_dict(cls, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> "Collection":
126        """
127        Build collection from dict
128        :param payload: dict payload
129        :returns: collection object
130        """
131        collection_id = str(payload.get("id", "")).strip()
132        title = str(payload.get("title", "")).strip()
133        if not collection_id:
134            raise ValueError("collection id is required")
135        if not title:
136            raise ValueError("collection title is required")
137
138        keywords = [str(k).strip() for k in payload.get("keywords", []) if str(k).strip()]
139        added_papers = [str(p).strip() for p in payload.get("added_papers", []) if str(p).strip()]
140
141        return cls(
142            id=collection_id,
143            title=title,
144            description=str(payload.get("description", "")).strip(),
145            keywords=keywords,
146            added_papers=added_papers,
147            created_at=str(payload.get("created_at", _utc_now_iso())),
148            updated_at=str(payload.get("updated_at", _utc_now_iso())),
149        )

Build collection from dict

Parameters
  • payload: dict payload :returns: collection object
class CollectionManager: 
 11class CollectionManager:
 12    """Manager for collection and json"""
 13
 14    def __init__(self, root_path: Path, collections_dir_name: str = "collections") -> None:
 15        """
 16        :param root_path: project root path
 17        :param collections_dir_name: target directory name for json files
 18        """
 19        self.root_path = Path(root_path)
 20        self.collections_path = self.root_path / collections_dir_name
 21        self.collections_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
 22
 23        self._collections: dict[str, Collection] = {}
 24        self.load_all()
 25
 26    def create_collection(
 27        self,
 28        title: str,
 29        description: str = "",
 30        keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None,
 31        collection_id: Optional[str] = None,
 32        autosave: bool = True,
 33    ) -> Collection:
 34        """
 35        Create and store a new collection
 36        :param title: collection title
 37        :param description: collection description
 38        :param keywords: optional list of keywords
 39        :param collection_id: optional custom id
 40        :param autosave: save json right after creation
 41        :returns: created collection object
 42        """
 43        normalised_title = str(title).strip()
 44        if not normalised_title:
 45            raise ValueError("collection title is required")
 46
 47        new_id = str(collection_id).strip() if collection_id else str(uuid4())
 48        if new_id in self._collections:
 49            raise ValueError(f'collection "{new_id}" already exists')
 50
 51        normalized_keywords = []
 52        for keyword in keywords or []:
 53            value = str(keyword).strip()
 54            if value and value not in normalized_keywords:
 55                normalized_keywords.append(value)
 56
 57        collection = Collection(
 58            id=new_id,
 59            title=normalised_title,
 60            description=str(description).strip(),
 61            keywords=normalized_keywords,
 62        )
 63        self._collections[new_id] = collection
 64
 65        if autosave:
 66            self.save_collection(new_id)
 67        return collection
 68
 69    def get_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> Collection:
 70        """
 71        Return collection by id or raise error
 72        :param collection_id: collection id
 73        :returns: collection object
 74        """
 75        if collection_id not in self._collections:
 76            raise KeyError(f'collection "{collection_id}" not found')
 77        return self._collections[str(collection_id).strip()]
 78
 79    def list_collections(self) -> list[Collection]:
 80        """
 81        Return all collections
 82        :returns: list of collections
 83        """
 84        return list(self._collections.values())
 85
 86    def delete_collection(self, collection_id: str, remove_file: bool = True) -> bool:
 87        """
 88        Delete collection from memory and optional file
 89        :param collection_id: collection id
 90        :param remove_file: remove json file if exists
 91        :returns: true if removed
 92        """
 93        normalized_id = str(collection_id).strip()
 94        if normalized_id not in self._collections:
 95            return False
 96
 97        del self._collections[normalized_id]
 98
 99        if remove_file:
100            file_path = self._collection_file_path(normalized_id)
101            if file_path.exists():
102                file_path.unlink()
103        return True
104
105    def add_paper(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
106        """
107        Add paper to collection
108        :param collection_id: collection id
109        :param paper_id: paper id
110        :param autosave: save json after update
111        :returns: true if added
112        """
113        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
114        changed = collection.add_paper(paper_id)
115        if changed and autosave:
116            self.save_collection(collection.id)
117        return changed
118
119    def remove_paper(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
120        """
121        Remove paper from collection
122        :param collection_id: collection id
123        :param paper_id: paper id
124        :param autosave: save json after update
125        :returns: true if removed
126        """
127        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
128        changed = collection.remove_paper(paper_id)
129        if changed and autosave:
130            self.save_collection(collection.id)
131        return changed
132
133    def add_keyword(self, collection_id: str, keyword: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
134        """
135        Add keyword to collection
136        :param collection_id: collection id
137        :param keyword: keyword text
138        :param autosave: save json after update
139        :returns: true if added
140        """
141        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
142        changed = collection.add_keyword(keyword)
143        if changed and autosave:
144            self.save_collection(collection.id)
145        return changed
146
147    def remove_keyword(self, collection_id: str, keyword: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
148        """
149        Remove keyword from collection
150        :param collection_id: collection id
151        :param keyword: keyword text
152        :param autosave: save json after update
153        :returns: true if removed
154        """
155        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
156        changed = collection.remove_keyword(keyword)
157        if changed and autosave:
158            self.save_collection(collection.id)
159        return changed
160
161    def change_title(self, collection_id: str, new_title: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
162        """
163        Update collection title
164        :param collection_id: collection id
165        :param new_title: new title
166        :param autosave: save json after update
167        :returns: true if changed
168        """
169        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
170        changed = collection.change_title(new_title)
171        if changed and autosave:
172            self.save_collection(collection.id)
173        return changed
174
175    def change_description(self, collection_id: str, new_description: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
176        """
177        Update collection description
178        :param collection_id: collection id
179        :param new_description: new description
180        :param autosave: save json after update
181        :returns: true if changed
182        """
183        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
184        changed = collection.change_description(new_description)
185        if changed and autosave:
186            self.save_collection(collection.id)
187        return changed
188
189    def save_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> Path:
190        """
191        Save one collection to json file
192        :param collection_id: collection id
193        :returns: path to saved file
194        """
195        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
196        file_path = self._collection_file_path(collection.id)
197
198        with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
199            json.dump(collection.to_dict(), f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
200        return file_path
201
202    def save_all_to_json(self) -> None:
203        """Save all loaded collections to json files"""
204        for collection in self._collections.values():
205            self.save_collection(collection.id)
206
207    def load_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> Collection:
208        """
209        Load one collection from json file
210        :param collection_id: collection id
211        :returns: loaded collection object
212        """
213        file_path = self._collection_file_path(collection_id)
214        if not file_path.exists():
215            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {file_path}")
216
217        with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
218            payload = json.load(f)
219
220        collection = Collection.from_dict(payload)
221        self._collections[collection.id] = collection
222        return collection
223
224    def load_all(self) -> None:
225        """Load all collections from json directory"""
226        self._collections.clear()
227        for file_path in sorted(self.collections_path.glob("*.json")):
228            with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
229                payload = json.load(f)
230            collection = Collection.from_dict(payload)
231            self._collections[collection.id] = collection
232
233    def get_profile_text(self, collection_id: str) -> str:
234        """
235        Build collection profile text for recommendation logic
236        :param collection_id: collection id
237        :returns: text from title, description and keywords
238        """
239        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
240
241        parts = [collection.title.strip(), collection.description.strip(), " ".join(collection.keywords).strip()]
242        return " ".join([part for part in parts if part]).strip()
243
244    def to_list_of_dicts(self) -> list[dict]:
245        """
246        Dump all collections to list of dict
247        :returns: list with serialized collections
248        """
249        return [collection.to_dict() for collection in self.list_collections()]
250
251    def _collection_file_path(self, collection_id: str) -> Path:
252        """
253        Build json file path for collection
254        :param collection_id: collection id
255        :returns: path to json
256        """
257        normalized_id = str(collection_id).strip()
258        if not normalized_id:
259            raise ValueError("collection id is required")
260        return self.collections_path / f"{normalized_id}.json"

Manager for collection and json

CollectionManager(root_path: pathlib.Path, collections_dir_name: str = 'collections') 
14    def __init__(self, root_path: Path, collections_dir_name: str = "collections") -> None:
15        """
16        :param root_path: project root path
17        :param collections_dir_name: target directory name for json files
18        """
19        self.root_path = Path(root_path)
20        self.collections_path = self.root_path / collections_dir_name
21        self.collections_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
22
23        self._collections: dict[str, Collection] = {}
24        self.load_all()

  • root_path: project root path

def create_collection( self, title: str, description: str = '', keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None, collection_id: Optional[str] = None, autosave: bool = True) -> Collection: 
26    def create_collection(
27        self,
28        title: str,
29        description: str = "",
30        keywords: Optional[list[str]] = None,
31        collection_id: Optional[str] = None,
32        autosave: bool = True,
33    ) -> Collection:
34        """
35        Create and store a new collection
36        :param title: collection title
37        :param description: collection description
38        :param keywords: optional list of keywords
39        :param collection_id: optional custom id
40        :param autosave: save json right after creation
41        :returns: created collection object
42        """
43        normalised_title = str(title).strip()
44        if not normalised_title:
45            raise ValueError("collection title is required")
46
47        new_id = str(collection_id).strip() if collection_id else str(uuid4())
48        if new_id in self._collections:
49            raise ValueError(f'collection "{new_id}" already exists')
50
51        normalized_keywords = []
52        for keyword in keywords or []:
53            value = str(keyword).strip()
54            if value and value not in normalized_keywords:
55                normalized_keywords.append(value)
56
57        collection = Collection(
58            id=new_id,
59            title=normalised_title,
60            description=str(description).strip(),
61            keywords=normalized_keywords,
62        )
63        self._collections[new_id] = collection
64
65        if autosave:
66            self.save_collection(new_id)
67        return collection

 86    def delete_collection(self, collection_id: str, remove_file: bool = True) -> bool:
 87        """
 88        Delete collection from memory and optional file
 89        :param collection_id: collection id
 90        :param remove_file: remove json file if exists
 91        :returns: true if removed
 92        """
 93        normalized_id = str(collection_id).strip()

106        """
107        Add paper to collection
108        :param collection_id: collection id
109        :param paper_id: paper id

112        """

def remove_paper(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool: 
119    def remove_paper(self, collection_id: str, paper_id: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
120        """
121        Remove paper from collection
122        :param collection_id: collection id
123        :param paper_id: paper id
124        :param autosave: save json after update
125        :returns: true if removed
126        """
127        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
128        changed = collection.remove_paper(paper_id)
129        if changed and autosave:
130            self.save_collection(collection.id)
131        return changed

Remove paper from collection

Parameters
  • collection_id: collection id
  • paper_id: paper id
  • autosave: save json after update :returns: true if removed
def add_keyword(self, collection_id: str, keyword: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool: 
133    def add_keyword(self, collection_id: str, keyword: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
134        """
135        Add keyword to collection
136        :param collection_id: collection id
137        :param keyword: keyword text
138        :param autosave: save json after update
139        :returns: true if added
140        """
141        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
142        changed = collection.add_keyword(keyword)
143        if changed and autosave:
144            self.save_collection(collection.id)

def remove_keyword(self, collection_id: str, keyword: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool: 
147    def remove_keyword(self, collection_id: str, keyword: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
148        """
149        Remove keyword from collection
150        :param collection_id: collection id
151        :param keyword: keyword text
152        :param autosave: save json after update
153        :returns: true if removed
154        """
155        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)

def change_title(self, collection_id: str, new_title: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool: 
161    def change_title(self, collection_id: str, new_title: str, autosave: bool = True) -> bool:
162        """
163        Update collection title
164        :param collection_id: collection id
165        :param new_title: new title
166        :param autosave: save json after update
167        :returns: true if changed
168        """
169        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)

176        """
177        Update collection description
178        :param collection_id: collection id
179        :param new_description: new description

181        :returns: true if changed

Parameters

def save_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> pathlib.Path: 
189    def save_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> Path:
190        """
191        Save one collection to json file
192        :param collection_id: collection id
193        :returns: path to saved file
194        """
195        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
196        file_path = self._collection_file_path(collection.id)
197
198        with open(file_path, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
199            json.dump(collection.to_dict(), f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
200        return file_path

Save one collection to json file

Parameters
  • collection_id: collection id :returns: path to saved file
def save_all_to_json(self) -> None: 
202    def save_all_to_json(self) -> None:
203        """Save all loaded collections to json files"""
204        for collection in self._collections.values():
205            self.save_collection(collection.id)

Save all loaded collections to json files

def load_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> Collection: 
207    def load_collection(self, collection_id: str) -> Collection:
208        """
209        Load one collection from json file
210        :param collection_id: collection id
211        :returns: loaded collection object
212        """
213        file_path = self._collection_file_path(collection_id)
214        if not file_path.exists():
215            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {file_path}")
216
217        with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
218            payload = json.load(f)
219
220        collection = Collection.from_dict(payload)
221        self._collections[collection.id] = collection
222        return collection

Load one collection from json file

Parameters
  • collection_id: collection id :returns: loaded collection object
def load_all(self) -> None: 
224    def load_all(self) -> None:
225        """Load all collections from json directory"""
226        self._collections.clear()
227        for file_path in sorted(self.collections_path.glob("*.json")):
228            with open(file_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
229                payload = json.load(f)
230            collection = Collection.from_dict(payload)
231            self._collections[collection.id] = collection

Load all collections from json directory

def get_profile_text(self, collection_id: str) -> str: 
233    def get_profile_text(self, collection_id: str) -> str:
234        """
235        Build collection profile text for recommendation logic
236        :param collection_id: collection id
237        :returns: text from title, description and keywords
238        """
239        collection = self.get_collection(collection_id)
240
241        parts = [collection.title.strip(), collection.description.strip(), " ".join(collection.keywords).strip()]
242        return " ".join([part for part in parts if part]).strip()

Build collection profile text for recommendation logic

Parameters
  • collection_id: collection id :returns: text from title, description and keywords
def to_list_of_dicts(self) -> list[dict]: 
244    def to_list_of_dicts(self) -> list[dict]:
245        """
246        Dump all collections to list of dict
247        :returns: list with serialized collections
248        """
249        return [collection.to_dict() for collection in self.list_collections()]

Dump all collections to list of dict :returns: list with serialized collections

class TextPreprocessor: 
 14class TextPreprocessor:
 15    additional_stop_words = [
 16        'study', 'research', 'method', 'result', 'findings', 'paper', 'theory', 'data',
 17        'approach', 'analysis', 'review', 'article'
 18    ]
 19
 20    def __init__(self):
 21        # get regex filters
 22        self.html_regex = re.compile(r'<[^>]*>')
 23        self.url_regex = re.compile(r'https?://\S+|www\.\S+')
 24        self.spacelike_regex = re.compile(r'\s+')
 25
 26        # initialise spaCy for English
 27        self.nlp = spacy.load("en_core_web_sm")
 28
 29        # stopwords
 30        self.stop_words = set(stopwords.words('english'))
 31        self.stop_words.update(self.additional_stop_words)
 32
 33    def _clean_noise(self, text: str) -> str:
 34        """
 35        Clean html-tags, url-links, multiple space-like chars
 36        :param text: text to be cleaned
 37        :return: preprocessed text
 38        """
 39        text = self.html_regex.sub(' ', text)
 40        text = self.url_regex.sub(' ', text)
 41        text = self.spacelike_regex.sub(' ', text)
 42        return text.strip().lower()
 43
 44    def process_string(self, text: str) -> str:
 45        if not isinstance(text, str) or not text:
 46            return ""
 47
 48        cleaned = self._clean_noise(text)
 49        if not cleaned:
 50            return ""
 51
 52        # spaCy lemmatization
 53        doc = self.nlp(cleaned)
 54        lemmatised_text = []
 55
 56        for token in doc:
 57            lemma = token.lemma_.lower().strip()
 58
 59            # skip spaces, punctuation and empty tokens
 60            if token.is_space or token.is_punct:
 61                continue
 62
 63            # skip stopwords
 64            if lemma in self.stop_words:
 65                continue
 66
 67            # skip empty / very short / non-alphabetic tokens
 68            if not lemma or len(lemma) < 1:
 69                continue
 70            if not any(char.isalpha() for char in lemma):
 71                continue
 72
 73            lemmatised_text.append(lemma)
 74
 75        return " ".join(lemmatised_text)
 76
 77    @staticmethod
 78    def detect_language(text: str) -> str:
 79        """ Detect language of the input text """
 80        try:
 81            return detect(text)
 82        except:
 83            return 'unknown'
 84
 85    def preprocessing_pipeline(self, input_df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
 86        # init parallel processing
 87        pandarallel.initialize(progress_bar=False)
 88
 89        input_df = input_df.copy()
 90
 91        # Detect language of title and abstract
 92        input_df['title_lang'] = input_df['title'].apply(self.detect_language)
 93        input_df['abstract_lang'] = input_df['abstract'].apply(self.detect_language)
 94
 95        # add column for cleaned and lemmatised title and abstract
 96        input_df['title_lemmatised'] = input_df['title'].parallel_apply(self.process_string)
 97        input_df['abstract_lemmatised'] = input_df['abstract'].parallel_apply(self.process_string)
 98
 99        # create index text based on language
100        input_df['index_text_lemmatised'] = input_df.apply(
101            lambda row: (
102                row['title_lemmatised'] + ' ' + row['abstract_lemmatised']
103                if row['title_lang'] == 'en' else row['abstract_lemmatised']
104            ),
105            axis=1
106        )
107
108        # clean empty title and abstract
109        input_df = input_df[
110            ~(
111                    (input_df['title_lemmatised'] == '') &
112                    (input_df['abstract_lemmatised'] == '')
113            )
114        ]
115
116        input_df = input_df.drop(columns=['title_lang', 'abstract_lang'])
117
118        return input_df.reset_index(drop=True)
additional_stop_words = ['study', 'research', 'method', 'result', 'findings', 'paper', 'theory', 'data', 'approach', 'analysis', 'review', 'article']
html_regex
url_regex
spacelike_regex
nlp
stop_words
def process_string(self, text: str) -> str: 
44    def process_string(self, text: str) -> str:
45        if not isinstance(text, str) or not text:
46            return ""
47
48        cleaned = self._clean_noise(text)
49        if not cleaned:
50            return ""
51
52        # spaCy lemmatization
53        doc = self.nlp(cleaned)
54        lemmatised_text = []
55
56        for token in doc:
57            lemma = token.lemma_.lower().strip()
58
59            # skip spaces, punctuation and empty tokens
60            if token.is_space or token.is_punct:
61                continue
62
63            # skip stopwords
64            if lemma in self.stop_words:
65                continue
66
67            # skip empty / very short / non-alphabetic tokens
68            if not lemma or len(lemma) < 1:
69                continue
70            if not any(char.isalpha() for char in lemma):
71                continue
72
73            lemmatised_text.append(lemma)
74
75        return " ".join(lemmatised_text)
@staticmethod
def detect_language(text: str) -> str: 
77    @staticmethod
78    def detect_language(text: str) -> str:
79        """ Detect language of the input text """
80        try:
81            return detect(text)
82        except:
83            return 'unknown'

Detect language of the input text

def preprocessing_pipeline( self, input_df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame) -> pandas.core.frame.DataFrame: 
 85    def preprocessing_pipeline(self, input_df: pd.DataFrame) -> pd.DataFrame:
 86        # init parallel processing
 87        pandarallel.initialize(progress_bar=False)
 88
 89        input_df = input_df.copy()
 90
 91        # Detect language of title and abstract
 92        input_df['title_lang'] = input_df['title'].apply(self.detect_language)
 93        input_df['abstract_lang'] = input_df['abstract'].apply(self.detect_language)
 94
 95        # add column for cleaned and lemmatised title and abstract
 96        input_df['title_lemmatised'] = input_df['title'].parallel_apply(self.process_string)
 97        input_df['abstract_lemmatised'] = input_df['abstract'].parallel_apply(self.process_string)
 98
 99        # create index text based on language
100        input_df['index_text_lemmatised'] = input_df.apply(
101            lambda row: (
102                row['title_lemmatised'] + ' ' + row['abstract_lemmatised']
103                if row['title_lang'] == 'en' else row['abstract_lemmatised']
104            ),
105            axis=1
106        )
107
108        # clean empty title and abstract
109        input_df = input_df[
110            ~(
111                    (input_df['title_lemmatised'] == '') &
112                    (input_df['abstract_lemmatised'] == '')
113            )
114        ]
115
116        input_df = input_df.drop(columns=['title_lang', 'abstract_lang'])
117
118        return input_df.reset_index(drop=True)
class BaseIndexer(abc.ABC): 
 7class BaseIndexer(ABC):
 8    @abstractmethod
 9    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
10        """
11        Build an internal index from preprocessed documents
12        :param texts: list of preprocessed documents
13        """
14
15    @abstractmethod
16    def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:
17        """
18        Score documents with the query
19        :param query_tokens: tokenised query
20        :returns: doc_id -> score mapping
21        """
22
23    @abstractmethod
24    def save(self, out_dir: Path) -> None:
25        """
26        Save index artifacts to given directory
27        :param out_dir: target dir where artifacts will be saved
28        """
29
30    @classmethod
31    @abstractmethod
32    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, **kwargs) -> "BaseIndexer":
33        """
34        Load index artifacts from given directory
35        :param in_dir: target dir with index artifacts
36        :returns: loaded index object instance
37        """

Helper class that provides a standard way to create an ABC using inheritance.

@abstractmethod
def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None: 
 8    @abstractmethod
 9    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
10        """
11        Build an internal index from preprocessed documents
12        :param texts: list of preprocessed documents
13        """

def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:

17        """
18        Score documents with the query
19        :param query_tokens: tokenised query
20        :returns: doc_id -> score mapping
21        """

25        """

27        :param out_dir: target dir where artifacts will be saved
28        """

def load(cls, in_dir: pathlib.Path, **kwargs) -> BaseIndexer: 
30    @classmethod
31    @abstractmethod
32    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, **kwargs) -> "BaseIndexer":
33        """
34        Load index artifacts from given directory
35        :param in_dir: target dir with index artifacts
36        :returns: loaded index object instance
37        """

Load index artifacts from given directory

class BM25Indexer(src.BaseIndexer): 
10class BM25Indexer(BaseIndexer):
11    """BM25 index by rank_bm25.BM25Okapi"""
12
13    def __init__(self) -> None:
14        self.model: BM25Okapi | None = None
15
16    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
17        """
18        Builds inverted document index by BM-25
19        :param texts: documents
20        :return: None
21        """
22        # tokenise for bm250kapi model (docs are already preprocessed earlier)
23        tokenized_corpus = [doc.split() for doc in texts]
24        self.model = BM25Okapi(tokenized_corpus)
25
26    def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:
27        """
28        Calculate scores by query tokens (BM-25)
29        :param query_tokens: list of query tokens
30        :return: doc -> score mapping
31        """
32        if self.model is None:
33            return {}
34
35        scores = self.model.get_scores(query_tokens)
36        return {i: float(s) for i, s in enumerate(scores) if s > 0}
37
38    def save(self, out_dir: Path) -> None:
39        """
40        Save bm25 index artifacts
41        :param out_dir: target directory to save index artifacts
42        """
43        # create target directory
44        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
45
46        # check if index is built inside object instance
47        if self.model is None:
48            raise ValueError("index is not built yet")
49
50        # dump binary pickle with BM250Kapi model
51        with open(out_dir / "bm25.pkl", "wb") as f:
52            pickle.dump(self.model, f)
53
54        # add meta information about created index
55        meta = {
56            "index_type": "bm25",
57        }
58        with open(out_dir / "meta.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
59            json.dump(meta, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
60
61    @classmethod
62    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, **kwargs) -> "BM25Indexer":
63        """
64        Load bm25 index artifacts
65        :param in_dir: target directory with index artifacts
66        :returns: loaded index object instance
67        """
68        instance = cls()
69
70        # target path to index dir check if exists
71        # load BM250Kapi index object
72        bm25_path = in_dir / "bm25.pkl"
73        if not bm25_path.exists():
74            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {bm25_path}")
75
76        with open(bm25_path, "rb") as f:
77            instance.model = pickle.load(f)
78
79        return instance

BM25 index by rank_bm25.BM25Okapi

model: rank_bm25.BM25Okapi | None
def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None: 
16    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
17        """
18        Builds inverted document index by BM-25
19        :param texts: documents
20        :return: None
21        """
22        # tokenise for bm250kapi model (docs are already preprocessed earlier)
23        tokenized_corpus = [doc.split() for doc in texts]
24        self.model = BM25Okapi(tokenized_corpus)

def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:

27        """
28        Calculate scores by query tokens (BM-25)

30        :return: doc -> score mapping
31        """
32        if self.model is None:
33            return {}
34
35        scores = self.model.get_scores(query_tokens)
36        return {i: float(s) for i, s in enumerate(scores) if s > 0}

def save(self, out_dir: pathlib.Path) -> None:

39        """
40        Save bm25 index artifacts
41        :param out_dir: target directory to save index artifacts
42        """
43        # create target directory
44        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
45
46        # check if index is built inside object instance
47        if self.model is None:
48            raise ValueError("index is not built yet")
49
50        # dump binary pickle with BM250Kapi model
51        with open(out_dir / "bm25.pkl", "wb") as f:
52            pickle.dump(self.model, f)
53
54        # add meta information about created index

56            "index_type": "bm25",
57        }

62    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, **kwargs) -> "BM25Indexer":
63        """
64        Load bm25 index artifacts
65        :param in_dir: target directory with index artifacts
66        :returns: loaded index object instance
67        """
68        instance = cls()
69
70        # target path to index dir check if exists
71        # load BM250Kapi index object
72        bm25_path = in_dir / "bm25.pkl"
73        if not bm25_path.exists():
74            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {bm25_path}")
75
76        with open(bm25_path, "rb") as f:
77            instance.model = pickle.load(f)
78
79        return instance

Load bm25 index artifacts

Parameters
  • in_dir: target directory with index artifacts :returns: loaded index object instance
class Word2VecIndexer(src.BaseIndexer): 
 12class Word2VecIndexer(BaseIndexer):
 13    """
 14    Indexer for word2vec model
 15    Document vector is a TF-IDF weighted mean of token vectors
 16    Query score is calculated by cosine similarity between normalised vectors
 17    """
 18
 19    def __init__(
 20            self,
 21            model: Any,
 22            tfidf_max_features: Optional[int] = 50_000,
 23            min_score: float = 0.0,
 24    ) -> None:
 25        """
 26        :param model: a word2vec-like model
 27        :param tfidf_max_features: maximum for TF-IDF vocabulary size
 28        :param min_score: minimum score to return
 29        """
 30        # initialise model and input
 31        self.model = model
 32        self.tfidf_max_features = tfidf_max_features
 33        self.min_score = min_score
 34
 35        # create future classes for TF-IDF vectors
 36        self.tfidf_vectorizer: Optional[TfidfVectorizer] = None
 37        self.feature_names: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
 38
 39        # initialise doc vectors of shape: (n_docs, dim)
 40        self.doc_vectors: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
 41
 42    def _get_token_vector(self, token: str) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
 43        """
 44        Internal function to extract vector from model
 45        :param token: target token
 46        :return: numpy array with token embedding
 47        """
 48        # check if model is not defined
 49        if not hasattr(self.model, "wv"):
 50            return None
 51
 52        # otherwise initialise wv
 53        wv = self.model.wv
 54
 55        # check if key -> index mapping exists in word2vec model
 56        if hasattr(wv, "key_to_index") and token not in wv.key_to_index:
 57            return None
 58        try:
 59            return np.asarray(wv[token], dtype=np.float32)
 60        except KeyError:
 61            return None
 62
 63    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
 64        """
 65        Function to create doc index, redefine self.doc_vectors
 66        :param texts: list of texts to preprocess
 67        :return: None
 68        """
 69        # create empty index if empty text list is given
 70        if not texts:
 71            # create doc zeros array with shape of word2vec model vector size
 72            self.doc_vectors = np.zeros((0, int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size", 0))), dtype=np.float32)
 73
 74            # create tokenizer
 75            self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 76                tokenizer=str.split, preprocessor=None, lowercase=False, max_features=self.tfidf_max_features
 77            )
 78            self.feature_names = np.array([], dtype=str)
 79            return
 80
 81        # create tf_idf matrix for given preprocessed texts
 82        self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 83            tokenizer=str.split,
 84            preprocessor=None,
 85            lowercase=False,
 86            max_features=self.tfidf_max_features,
 87        )
 88        tfidf_matrix = self.tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(texts)
 89        self.feature_names = np.asarray(self.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
 90
 91        # create doc vector matrix
 92        dim = int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size"))
 93        doc_vectors = np.zeros((len(texts), dim), dtype=np.float32)
 94
 95        # for each doc add vector of each token
 96        for doc_id in range(len(texts)):
 97            row = tfidf_matrix.getrow(doc_id)
 98            vec = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float32)
 99            weight_sum = 0.0
100
101            # use only tokens with non-zero tf-idf weight
102            for feature_idx, weight in zip(row.indices, row.data):
103                token = str(self.feature_names[feature_idx])
104                token_vec = self._get_token_vector(token)
105                if token_vec is None:
106                    continue
107                vec += float(weight) * token_vec
108                weight_sum += float(weight)
109
110            if weight_sum > 0:
111                vec /= weight_sum
112
113            # normalise, cosine similarity is dot product
114            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vec))
115            if norm > 0:
116                vec /= norm
117
118            doc_vectors[doc_id] = vec
119
120        self.doc_vectors = doc_vectors
121
122    def _build_query_vector(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
123        """
124        Internal function to create vector for query tokens
125        :param query_tokens: list of target words from query
126        :return: None (if query vector is empty) or target query vector (numpy-array)
127        """
128        # if something is not defined
129        if self.tfidf_vectorizer is None or self.feature_names is None:
130            return None
131        if not query_tokens:
132            return None
133
134        # join list of tokens
135        q_text = " ".join(query_tokens)
136
137        # get tfidf vector for query
138        q_tfidf = self.tfidf_vectorizer.transform([q_text])
139        row = q_tfidf.getrow(0)
140
141        # calculate target vector for query based on fasttext vectors of words and weighted tf-idf sum
142        dim = int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size"))
143        vec = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float32)
144        weight_sum = 0.0
145
146        for feature_idx, weight in zip(row.indices, row.data):
147            token = str(self.feature_names[feature_idx])
148            token_vec = self._get_token_vector(token)
149            if token_vec is None:
150                continue
151            vec += float(weight) * token_vec
152            weight_sum += float(weight)
153
154        if weight_sum > 0:
155            vec /= weight_sum
156
157        norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vec))
158        if norm > 0:
159            vec /= norm
160
161        # if total vector is empty then return None
162        if not np.any(vec):
163            return None
164        return vec
165
166    def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:
167        """
168        Calculate score for documents by given query
169        :param query_tokens: list of target tokens
170        :return: doc_id -> score mapping
171        """
172        # if index is empty
173        if self.doc_vectors is None:
174            return {}
175
176        # build query vector
177        q_vec = self._build_query_vector(query_tokens)
178        if q_vec is None:
179            return {}
180
181        # dot production to get cosine similarity
182        scores = self.doc_vectors @ q_vec
183
184        # filter scores by min_score if defined
185        if self.min_score is not None:
186            keep = scores > self.min_score
187            doc_ids = np.nonzero(keep)[0]
188        else:
189            doc_ids = np.arange(scores.shape[0])
190
191        # return scores by doc
192        return {int(doc_id): float(scores[doc_id]) for doc_id in doc_ids}
193
194    def save(self, out_dir: Path) -> None:
195        """
196        Save index artifacts for word2vec
197        :param out_dir: directory where index artifacts will be saved.
198        """
199        # check if dir exists and create it
200        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
201
202        # raise ValueError if some core vars of index are not defined
203        if self.doc_vectors is None or self.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
204            raise ValueError("index is not built yet")
205
206        # save npy with vectors
207        np.save(out_dir / "doc_vectors.npy", self.doc_vectors)
208
209        # save trained tfidf vectorizer in binary file
210        with open(out_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "wb") as f:
211            pickle.dump(self.tfidf_vectorizer, f)
212
213        # save metadata with params of indexer
214        dim = int(self.doc_vectors.shape[1]) if self.doc_vectors.ndim == 2 else 0
215        meta = {
216            "index_type": "word2vec",
217            "vector_dim": dim,
218            "tfidf_max_features": self.tfidf_max_features,
219            "min_score": self.min_score,
220        }
221
222        # save meta json
223        with open(out_dir / "meta.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
224            json.dump(meta, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
225
226    @classmethod
227    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, model: Any, **kwargs) -> "Word2VecIndexer":
228        """
229        Load index artifacts for fasttext
230        :param in_dir: directory with index artifacts
231        :param model: word2vec-like embedding model (used for query vectors)
232        :returns: loaded index instance
233        """
234        # check if meta information for right loading exists
235        meta_path = in_dir / "meta.json"
236        if not meta_path.exists():
237            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {meta_path}")
238
239        # load metadata if exists
240        with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
241            meta = json.load(f)
242
243        tfidf_max_features = meta.get("tfidf_max_features", 50_000)
244        min_score = meta.get("min_score", 0.0)
245
246        # create instance of class
247        instance = cls(model=model, tfidf_max_features=tfidf_max_features, min_score=min_score)
248
249        # load vectors
250        vectors_path = in_dir / "doc_vectors.npy"
251        if not vectors_path.exists():
252            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {vectors_path}")
253        instance.doc_vectors = np.load(vectors_path).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
254
255        # load trained tf-idf vectorizer
256        with open(in_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "rb") as f:
257            instance.tfidf_vectorizer = pickle.load(f)
258
259        # check if tf-idf is actually trained
260        if instance.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
261            raise ValueError("tfidf vectorizer is empty after loading")
262
263        instance.feature_names = np.asarray(instance.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
264        return instance

Indexer for word2vec model Document vector is a TF-IDF weighted mean of token vectors Query score is calculated by cosine similarity between normalised vectors

Word2VecIndexer( model: Any, tfidf_max_features: Optional[int] = 50000, min_score: float = 0.0) 
19    def __init__(
20            self,
21            model: Any,
22            tfidf_max_features: Optional[int] = 50_000,
23            min_score: float = 0.0,
24    ) -> None:
25        """
26        :param model: a word2vec-like model
27        :param tfidf_max_features: maximum for TF-IDF vocabulary size
28        :param min_score: minimum score to return
29        """
30        # initialise model and input
31        self.model = model
32        self.tfidf_max_features = tfidf_max_features
33        self.min_score = min_score
34
35        # create future classes for TF-IDF vectors
36        self.tfidf_vectorizer: Optional[TfidfVectorizer] = None
37        self.feature_names: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
38
39        # initialise doc vectors of shape: (n_docs, dim)
40        self.doc_vectors: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
Parameters
  • model: a word2vec-like model
  • tfidf_max_features: maximum for TF-IDF vocabulary size
  • min_score: minimum score to return
model
tfidf_max_features
min_score
tfidf_vectorizer: Optional[sklearn.feature_extraction.text.TfidfVectorizer]
feature_names: Optional[numpy.ndarray]
doc_vectors: Optional[numpy.ndarray]
def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None: 
 63    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
 64        """
 65        Function to create doc index, redefine self.doc_vectors
 66        :param texts: list of texts to preprocess
 67        :return: None
 68        """
 69        # create empty index if empty text list is given
 70        if not texts:
 71            # create doc zeros array with shape of word2vec model vector size
 72            self.doc_vectors = np.zeros((0, int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size", 0))), dtype=np.float32)
 73
 74            # create tokenizer
 75            self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 76                tokenizer=str.split, preprocessor=None, lowercase=False, max_features=self.tfidf_max_features
 77            )
 78            self.feature_names = np.array([], dtype=str)
 79            return
 80
 81        # create tf_idf matrix for given preprocessed texts
 82        self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 83            tokenizer=str.split,
 84            preprocessor=None,
 85            lowercase=False,
 86            max_features=self.tfidf_max_features,
 87        )
 88        tfidf_matrix = self.tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(texts)
 89        self.feature_names = np.asarray(self.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
 90
 91        # create doc vector matrix
 92        dim = int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size"))
 93        doc_vectors = np.zeros((len(texts), dim), dtype=np.float32)
 94
 95        # for each doc add vector of each token
 96        for doc_id in range(len(texts)):
 97            row = tfidf_matrix.getrow(doc_id)
 98            vec = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float32)
 99            weight_sum = 0.0
100
101            # use only tokens with non-zero tf-idf weight
102            for feature_idx, weight in zip(row.indices, row.data):
103                token = str(self.feature_names[feature_idx])
104                token_vec = self._get_token_vector(token)
105                if token_vec is None:
106                    continue
107                vec += float(weight) * token_vec
108                weight_sum += float(weight)
109
110            if weight_sum > 0:
111                vec /= weight_sum
112
113            # normalise, cosine similarity is dot product
114            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vec))
115            if norm > 0:
116                vec /= norm
117
118            doc_vectors[doc_id] = vec
119
120        self.doc_vectors = doc_vectors

Function to create doc index, redefine self.doc_vectors

Parameters
  • texts: list of texts to preprocess
Returns

None

def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]: 
166    def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:
167        """
168        Calculate score for documents by given query
169        :param query_tokens: list of target tokens
170        :return: doc_id -> score mapping
171        """
172        # if index is empty
173        if self.doc_vectors is None:
174            return {}
175
176        # build query vector
177        q_vec = self._build_query_vector(query_tokens)
178        if q_vec is None:
179            return {}
180
181        # dot production to get cosine similarity
182        scores = self.doc_vectors @ q_vec
183
184        # filter scores by min_score if defined
185        if self.min_score is not None:
186            keep = scores > self.min_score
187            doc_ids = np.nonzero(keep)[0]
188        else:
189            doc_ids = np.arange(scores.shape[0])
190
191        # return scores by doc
192        return {int(doc_id): float(scores[doc_id]) for doc_id in doc_ids}
