Returns

doc_id -> score mapping

def save(self, out_dir: pathlib.Path) -> None: 
194    def save(self, out_dir: Path) -> None:
195        """
196        Save index artifacts for word2vec
197        :param out_dir: directory where index artifacts will be saved.
198        """
199        # check if dir exists and create it
200        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
201
202        # raise ValueError if some core vars of index are not defined
203        if self.doc_vectors is None or self.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
204            raise ValueError("index is not built yet")
205
206        # save npy with vectors
207        np.save(out_dir / "doc_vectors.npy", self.doc_vectors)
208
209        # save trained tfidf vectorizer in binary file
210        with open(out_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "wb") as f:
211            pickle.dump(self.tfidf_vectorizer, f)
212
213        # save metadata with params of indexer
214        dim = int(self.doc_vectors.shape[1]) if self.doc_vectors.ndim == 2 else 0
215        meta = {
216            "index_type": "word2vec",
217            "vector_dim": dim,
218            "tfidf_max_features": self.tfidf_max_features,
219            "min_score": self.min_score,
220        }
221
222        # save meta json
223        with open(out_dir / "meta.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
224            json.dump(meta, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)

Save index artifacts for word2vec

Parameters
  • out_dir: directory where index artifacts will be saved.
@classmethod
def load( cls, in_dir: pathlib.Path, model: Any, **kwargs) -> Word2VecIndexer: 
226    @classmethod
227    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, model: Any, **kwargs) -> "Word2VecIndexer":
228        """
229        Load index artifacts for fasttext
230        :param in_dir: directory with index artifacts
231        :param model: word2vec-like embedding model (used for query vectors)
232        :returns: loaded index instance
233        """
234        # check if meta information for right loading exists
235        meta_path = in_dir / "meta.json"
236        if not meta_path.exists():
237            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {meta_path}")
238
239        # load metadata if exists
240        with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
241            meta = json.load(f)
242
243        tfidf_max_features = meta.get("tfidf_max_features", 50_000)
244        min_score = meta.get("min_score", 0.0)
245
246        # create instance of class
247        instance = cls(model=model, tfidf_max_features=tfidf_max_features, min_score=min_score)
248
249        # load vectors
250        vectors_path = in_dir / "doc_vectors.npy"
251        if not vectors_path.exists():
252            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {vectors_path}")
253        instance.doc_vectors = np.load(vectors_path).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
254
255        # load trained tf-idf vectorizer
256        with open(in_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "rb") as f:
257            instance.tfidf_vectorizer = pickle.load(f)
258
259        # check if tf-idf is actually trained
260        if instance.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
261            raise ValueError("tfidf vectorizer is empty after loading")
262
263        instance.feature_names = np.asarray(instance.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
264        return instance

Load index artifacts for fasttext

Parameters
  • in_dir: directory with index artifacts
  • model: word2vec-like embedding model (used for query vectors) :returns: loaded index instance
class FastTextIndexer(src.BaseIndexer): 
 12class FastTextIndexer(BaseIndexer):
 13    """Indexer for fasttext model"""
 14
 15    def __init__(
 16        self,
 17        model: Any,
 18        tfidf_max_features: Optional[int] = 50_000,
 19        min_score: float = 0.0,
 20    ) -> None:
 21        """
 22        :param model: a fasttext-like model
 23        :param tfidf_max_features: maximum for TF-IDF vocabulary size
 24        :param min_score: minimum score to return
 25        """
 26        # initialise model and input
 27        self.model = model
 28        self.tfidf_max_features = tfidf_max_features
 29        self.min_score = min_score
 30
 31        # create future classes for TF-IDF vectors
 32        self.tfidf_vectorizer: Optional[TfidfVectorizer] = None
 33        self.feature_names: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
 34
 35        # initialise doc vectors of shape: (n_docs, dim)
 36        self.doc_vectors: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
 37
 38    def _get_token_vector(self, token: str) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
 39        """
 40        Internal function to extract vector from model
 41        :param token: target token
 42        :return: numpy array with token embedding
 43        """
 44        # check if model is not defined
 45        if not hasattr(self.model, "wv"):
 46            return None
 47        # otherwise initialise wv
 48        wv = self.model.wv
 49
 50        try:
 51            # fasttext can sometimes produce vectors via subwords.
 52            return np.asarray(wv.get_vector(token), dtype=np.float32)
 53        except (KeyError, AttributeError):
 54            return None
 55
 56    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
 57        """
 58        Function to create doc index, redefine self.doc_vectors
 59        :param texts: list of texts to preprocess
 60        :return: None
 61        """
 62        # create empty index if empty text list is given
 63        if not texts:
 64            # create doc zeros array with shape of fasttext model vector size
 65            self.doc_vectors = np.zeros((0, int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size", 0))), dtype=np.float32)
 66
 67            # create tokenizer
 68            self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 69                tokenizer=str.split, preprocessor=None, lowercase=False, max_features=self.tfidf_max_features
 70            )
 71            self.feature_names = np.array([], dtype=str)
 72            return
 73
 74        # create tf_idf matrix for given preprocessed texts
 75        self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 76            tokenizer=str.split,
 77            preprocessor=None,
 78            lowercase=False,
 79            max_features=self.tfidf_max_features,
 80        )
 81        tfidf_matrix = self.tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(texts)
 82        self.feature_names = np.asarray(self.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
 83
 84        # create doc vector matrix
 85        dim = int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size"))
 86        doc_vectors = np.zeros((len(texts), dim), dtype=np.float32)
 87
 88        # for each doc add vector of each token
 89        for doc_id in range(len(texts)):
 90            row = tfidf_matrix.getrow(doc_id)
 91            vec = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float32)
 92            weight_sum = 0.0
 93
 94            # for each token render fasttext embeddings and add to weight sum
 95            for feature_idx, weight in zip(row.indices, row.data):
 96                token = str(self.feature_names[feature_idx])
 97                token_vec = self._get_token_vector(token)
 98                if token_vec is None:
 99                    continue
100                vec += float(weight) * token_vec
101                weight_sum += float(weight)
102
103            if weight_sum > 0:
104                vec /= weight_sum
105
106            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vec))
107            if norm > 0:
108                vec /= norm
109
110            doc_vectors[doc_id] = vec
111
112        self.doc_vectors = doc_vectors
113
114    def _build_query_vector(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> Optional[np.ndarray]:
115        """
116        Internal function to create vector for query tokens
117        :param query_tokens: list of target words from query
118        :return: None (if query vector is empty) or target query vector (numpy-array)
119        """
120        # if something is not defined
121        if self.tfidf_vectorizer is None or self.feature_names is None:
122            return None
123        if not query_tokens:
124            return None
125
126        # join list of tokens
127        q_text = " ".join(query_tokens)
128        # get tfidf vector for query
129        q_tfidf = self.tfidf_vectorizer.transform([q_text])
130        row = q_tfidf.getrow(0)
131
132        # calculate target vector for query based on fasttext vectors of words and weighted tf-idf sum
133        dim = int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size"))
134        vec = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float32)
135        weight_sum = 0.0
136
137        for feature_idx, weight in zip(row.indices, row.data):
138            token = str(self.feature_names[feature_idx])
139            token_vec = self._get_token_vector(token)
140            if token_vec is None:
141                continue
142            vec += float(weight) * token_vec
143            weight_sum += float(weight)
144
145        if weight_sum > 0:
146            vec /= weight_sum
147
148        norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vec))
149        if norm > 0:
150            vec /= norm
151
152        # if total vector is empty then return None
153        if not np.any(vec):
154            return None
155        return vec
156
157    def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:
158        """
159        Calculate score for documents by given query
160        :param query_tokens: list of target tokens
161        :return: doc_id -> score mapping
162        """
163        # if index is empty
164        if self.doc_vectors is None:
165            return {}
166
167        # build query vector
168        q_vec = self._build_query_vector(query_tokens)
169        if q_vec is None:
170            return {}
171
172        # dot production to get cosine similarity
173        scores = self.doc_vectors @ q_vec
174
175        # filter scores by min_score if defined
176        if self.min_score is not None:
177            keep = scores > self.min_score
178            doc_ids = np.nonzero(keep)[0]
179        else:
180            doc_ids = np.arange(scores.shape[0])
181
182        # return scores by doc
183        return {int(doc_id): float(scores[doc_id]) for doc_id in doc_ids}
184
185    def save(self, out_dir: Path) -> None:
186        """
187        Save index artifacts for fasttext
188        :param out_dir: directory where index artifacts will be saved.
189        """
190        # check if dir exists and create it
191        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
192
193        # rase ValueError if some core vars of index are not defined
194        if self.doc_vectors is None or self.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
195            raise ValueError("index is not built yet")
196
197        # save npy with vectors
198        np.save(out_dir / "doc_vectors.npy", self.doc_vectors)
199
200        # save trained tfidf vectorizer in binary file
201        with open(out_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "wb") as f:
202            pickle.dump(self.tfidf_vectorizer, f)
203
204        # save metadata with params of indexer
205        dim = int(self.doc_vectors.shape[1]) if self.doc_vectors.ndim == 2 else 0
206        meta = {
207            "index_type": "fasttext",
208            "vector_dim": dim,
209            "tfidf_max_features": self.tfidf_max_features,
210            "min_score": self.min_score,
211        }
212        # save meta json
213        with open(out_dir / "meta.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
214            json.dump(meta, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
215
216    @classmethod
217    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, model: Any, **kwargs) -> "FastTextIndexer":
218        """
219        Load index artifacts for fasttext
220        :param in_dir: directory with index artifacts
221        :param model: fasttext-like embedding model (used for query vectors)
222        :returns: loaded index instance
223        """
224        # check if meta information for right loading exists
225        meta_path = in_dir / "meta.json"
226        if not meta_path.exists():
227            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {meta_path}")
228
229        # load metadata if exists
230        with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
231            meta = json.load(f)
232
233        tfidf_max_features = meta["tfidf_max_features"] if "tfidf_max_features" in meta else 50000
234        min_score = meta["min_score"] if "min_score" in meta else 0.0
235
236        # create instance of class
237        instance = cls(model=model, tfidf_max_features=tfidf_max_features, min_score=min_score)
238
239        # load vectors
240        vectors_path = in_dir / "doc_vectors.npy"
241        if not vectors_path.exists():
242            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {vectors_path}")
243        instance.doc_vectors = np.load(vectors_path).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
244
245        # load trained tf-idf vectorizer
246        with open(in_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "rb") as f:
247            instance.tfidf_vectorizer = pickle.load(f)
248
249        # check if tf-idf is actually trained
250        if instance.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
251            raise ValueError("TF-IDF vectorizer is empty after loading")
252
253        instance.feature_names = np.asarray(instance.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
254        return instance

Indexer for fasttext model

FastTextIndexer( model: Any, tfidf_max_features: Optional[int] = 50000, min_score: float = 0.0) 
15    def __init__(
16        self,
17        model: Any,
18        tfidf_max_features: Optional[int] = 50_000,
19        min_score: float = 0.0,
20    ) -> None:
21        """
22        :param model: a fasttext-like model
23        :param tfidf_max_features: maximum for TF-IDF vocabulary size
24        :param min_score: minimum score to return
25        """
26        # initialise model and input
27        self.model = model
28        self.tfidf_max_features = tfidf_max_features
29        self.min_score = min_score
30
31        # create future classes for TF-IDF vectors
32        self.tfidf_vectorizer: Optional[TfidfVectorizer] = None
33        self.feature_names: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
34
35        # initialise doc vectors of shape: (n_docs, dim)
36        self.doc_vectors: Optional[np.ndarray] = None
Parameters
  • model: a fasttext-like model
  • tfidf_max_features: maximum for TF-IDF vocabulary size
  • min_score: minimum score to return
model
tfidf_max_features
min_score
tfidf_vectorizer: Optional[sklearn.feature_extraction.text.TfidfVectorizer]
feature_names: Optional[numpy.ndarray]
doc_vectors: Optional[numpy.ndarray]
def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None: 
 56    def build_index(self, texts: list[str]) -> None:
 57        """
 58        Function to create doc index, redefine self.doc_vectors
 59        :param texts: list of texts to preprocess
 60        :return: None
 61        """
 62        # create empty index if empty text list is given
 63        if not texts:
 64            # create doc zeros array with shape of fasttext model vector size
 65            self.doc_vectors = np.zeros((0, int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size", 0))), dtype=np.float32)
 66
 67            # create tokenizer
 68            self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 69                tokenizer=str.split, preprocessor=None, lowercase=False, max_features=self.tfidf_max_features
 70            )
 71            self.feature_names = np.array([], dtype=str)
 72            return
 73
 74        # create tf_idf matrix for given preprocessed texts
 75        self.tfidf_vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(
 76            tokenizer=str.split,
 77            preprocessor=None,
 78            lowercase=False,
 79            max_features=self.tfidf_max_features,
 80        )
 81        tfidf_matrix = self.tfidf_vectorizer.fit_transform(texts)
 82        self.feature_names = np.asarray(self.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
 83
 84        # create doc vector matrix
 85        dim = int(getattr(self.model, "vector_size"))
 86        doc_vectors = np.zeros((len(texts), dim), dtype=np.float32)
 87
 88        # for each doc add vector of each token
 89        for doc_id in range(len(texts)):
 90            row = tfidf_matrix.getrow(doc_id)
 91            vec = np.zeros(dim, dtype=np.float32)
 92            weight_sum = 0.0
 93
 94            # for each token render fasttext embeddings and add to weight sum
 95            for feature_idx, weight in zip(row.indices, row.data):
 96                token = str(self.feature_names[feature_idx])
 97                token_vec = self._get_token_vector(token)
 98                if token_vec is None:
 99                    continue
100                vec += float(weight) * token_vec
101                weight_sum += float(weight)
102
103            if weight_sum > 0:
104                vec /= weight_sum
105
106            norm = float(np.linalg.norm(vec))
107            if norm > 0:
108                vec /= norm
109
110            doc_vectors[doc_id] = vec
111
112        self.doc_vectors = doc_vectors

Function to create doc index, redefine self.doc_vectors

Parameters
  • texts: list of texts to preprocess
Returns

None

def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]: 
157    def get_scores(self, query_tokens: list[str]) -> dict[int, float]:
158        """
159        Calculate score for documents by given query
160        :param query_tokens: list of target tokens
161        :return: doc_id -> score mapping
162        """
163        # if index is empty
164        if self.doc_vectors is None:
165            return {}
166
167        # build query vector
168        q_vec = self._build_query_vector(query_tokens)
169        if q_vec is None:
170            return {}
171
172        # dot production to get cosine similarity
173        scores = self.doc_vectors @ q_vec
174
175        # filter scores by min_score if defined
176        if self.min_score is not None:
177            keep = scores > self.min_score
178            doc_ids = np.nonzero(keep)[0]
179        else:
180            doc_ids = np.arange(scores.shape[0])
181
182        # return scores by doc
183        return {int(doc_id): float(scores[doc_id]) for doc_id in doc_ids}

Calculate score for documents by given query

Parameters
  • query_tokens: list of target tokens
Returns

doc_id -> score mapping

def save(self, out_dir: pathlib.Path) -> None: 
185    def save(self, out_dir: Path) -> None:
186        """
187        Save index artifacts for fasttext
188        :param out_dir: directory where index artifacts will be saved.
189        """
190        # check if dir exists and create it
191        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
192
193        # rase ValueError if some core vars of index are not defined
194        if self.doc_vectors is None or self.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
195            raise ValueError("index is not built yet")
196
197        # save npy with vectors
198        np.save(out_dir / "doc_vectors.npy", self.doc_vectors)
199
200        # save trained tfidf vectorizer in binary file
201        with open(out_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "wb") as f:
202            pickle.dump(self.tfidf_vectorizer, f)
203
204        # save metadata with params of indexer
205        dim = int(self.doc_vectors.shape[1]) if self.doc_vectors.ndim == 2 else 0
206        meta = {
207            "index_type": "fasttext",
208            "vector_dim": dim,
209            "tfidf_max_features": self.tfidf_max_features,
210            "min_score": self.min_score,
211        }
212        # save meta json
213        with open(out_dir / "meta.json", "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
214            json.dump(meta, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)

Save index artifacts for fasttext

Parameters
  • out_dir: directory where index artifacts will be saved.
@classmethod
def load( cls, in_dir: pathlib.Path, model: Any, **kwargs) -> FastTextIndexer: 
216    @classmethod
217    def load(cls, in_dir: Path, model: Any, **kwargs) -> "FastTextIndexer":
218        """
219        Load index artifacts for fasttext
220        :param in_dir: directory with index artifacts
221        :param model: fasttext-like embedding model (used for query vectors)
222        :returns: loaded index instance
223        """
224        # check if meta information for right loading exists
225        meta_path = in_dir / "meta.json"
226        if not meta_path.exists():
227            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {meta_path}")
228
229        # load metadata if exists
230        with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
231            meta = json.load(f)
232
233        tfidf_max_features = meta["tfidf_max_features"] if "tfidf_max_features" in meta else 50000
234        min_score = meta["min_score"] if "min_score" in meta else 0.0
235
236        # create instance of class
237        instance = cls(model=model, tfidf_max_features=tfidf_max_features, min_score=min_score)
238
239        # load vectors
240        vectors_path = in_dir / "doc_vectors.npy"
241        if not vectors_path.exists():
242            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {vectors_path}")
243        instance.doc_vectors = np.load(vectors_path).astype(np.float32, copy=False)
244
245        # load trained tf-idf vectorizer
246        with open(in_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl", "rb") as f:
247            instance.tfidf_vectorizer = pickle.load(f)
248
249        # check if tf-idf is actually trained
250        if instance.tfidf_vectorizer is None:
251            raise ValueError("TF-IDF vectorizer is empty after loading")
252
253        instance.feature_names = np.asarray(instance.tfidf_vectorizer.get_feature_names_out())
254        return instance

Load index artifacts for fasttext

Parameters
  • in_dir: directory with index artifacts
  • model: fasttext-like embedding model (used for query vectors) :returns: loaded index instance
class W2VWrapper:

270        self.vector_size = kv.vector_size
W2VWrapper(kv)

270        self.vector_size = kv.vector_size
wv
vector_size
class SearchEngine: 
 2class SearchEngine:
 3    def __init__(self, indexer, preprocessor):
 4        """
 5        Initialisation of search engine
 6        :param indexer: class of indexer to search with
 7        :param preprocessor: class of text preprocessor to preprocess queries
 8        """
 9        # search engine takes an indexer and query preprocessor
10        # define indexer from /src/indexers
11        self.indexer = indexer
12
13        # preprocessor (our main is in /src/preprocessing.py)
14        self.preprocessor = preprocessor
15
16        # var for storing text without preprocessing
17        self.original_texts, self.processed_texts = [], []
18
19    def fit(self, original_texts: list[str], processed_texts: list[str]) -> None:
20        """
21        Function to add text and create index based on it
22        :param original_texts: original texts from dataset
23        :param processed_texts: preprocessed texts
24        :return: None
25        """
26        # store original texts without preprocessing
27        self.original_texts, self.processed_texts = original_texts, processed_texts
28
29        # build index from already preprocessed data
30        self.indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
31
32    def search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[dict]:
33        """
34        Main function to search string based
35        :param query: str text to search
36        :param top_k: number of documents by score to return
37        :return: list of relevant documents by score
38        """
39        # preprocess query to match index terms
40        query_tokens = self.preprocessor.process_string(query).split()
41
42        if not query_tokens:
43            return []
44
45        # get scores from indexer
46        scores = self.indexer.get_scores(query_tokens)
47
48        # rank documents by score in descending sort
49        ranked_ids = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
50
51        results = []
52        # for each top_k doc save it to results with score and original text
53        for doc_id, score in ranked_ids[:top_k]:
54            results.append({
55                "id": doc_id,
56                "score": round(score, 4),
57                "text": self.original_texts[doc_id],
58                "preprocessed_text": self.processed_texts[doc_id]
59            })
60
61        return results
SearchEngine(indexer, preprocessor) 
 3    def __init__(self, indexer, preprocessor):
 4        """
 5        Initialisation of search engine
 6        :param indexer: class of indexer to search with
 7        :param preprocessor: class of text preprocessor to preprocess queries
 8        """
 9        # search engine takes an indexer and query preprocessor
10        # define indexer from /src/indexers
11        self.indexer = indexer
12
13        # preprocessor (our main is in /src/preprocessing.py)
14        self.preprocessor = preprocessor
15
16        # var for storing text without preprocessing
17        self.original_texts, self.processed_texts = [], []

Initialisation of search engine

  • indexer: class of indexer to search with
  • preprocessor: class of text preprocessor to preprocess queries
indexer
preprocessor
def fit(self, original_texts: list[str], processed_texts: list[str]) -> None:

20        """
21        Function to add text and create index based on it
22        :param original_texts: original texts from dataset
23        :param processed_texts: preprocessed texts
24        :return: None
25        """
26        # store original texts without preprocessing
27        self.original_texts, self.processed_texts = original_texts, processed_texts
28
29        # build index from already preprocessed data
30        self.indexer.build_index(processed_texts)

Parameters
  • original_texts: original texts from dataset
  • processed_texts: preprocessed texts
Returns

None

def search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[dict]: 
32    def search(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5) -> list[dict]:
33        """
34        Main function to search string based
35        :param query: str text to search
36        :param top_k: number of documents by score to return
37        :return: list of relevant documents by score
38        """
39        # preprocess query to match index terms
40        query_tokens = self.preprocessor.process_string(query).split()
41
42        if not query_tokens:
43            return []
44
45        # get scores from indexer
46        scores = self.indexer.get_scores(query_tokens)
47
48        # rank documents by score in descending sort
49        ranked_ids = sorted(scores.items(), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
50
51        results = []
52        # for each top_k doc save it to results with score and original text
53        for doc_id, score in ranked_ids[:top_k]:
54            results.append({
55                "id": doc_id,
56                "score": round(score, 4),
57                "text": self.original_texts[doc_id],
58                "preprocessed_text": self.processed_texts[doc_id]
59            })
60
61        return results

list of relevant documents by score

class SearchEngineFactory: 
 10class SearchEngineFactory:
 11    """Create and cache SearchEngine objects for different indexers"""
 12
 13    SUPPORTED_INDEXES = ("bm25", "word2vec", "fasttext")
 14
 15    def __init__(
 16            self,
 17            indexes_root: Path,
 18            preprocessor,
 19            original_texts: list[str],
 20            processed_texts: list[str],
 21            models: dict,
 22    ):
 23        """
 24        :param indexes_root: root directory with index artifacts
 25        :param preprocessor: text preprocessor to preprocess queries
 26        :param original_texts: original corpus texts for output
 27        :param processed_texts: preprocessed corpus texts for searching
 28        :param models: embedding models if needed, e.g. {"word2vec": w2v_model, "fasttext": ft_model}.
 29        """
 30        # check if preprocessed texts and original ones differ
 31        if len(original_texts) != len(processed_texts):
 32            raise ValueError("original_texts and processed_texts must have equal length")
 33
 34        # initialise main vars for indexers
 35        self.indexes_root = Path(indexes_root)
 36
 37        # define preprocessor module
 38        self.preprocessor = preprocessor
 39
 40        # initialise data
 41        self.original_texts = original_texts
 42        self.processed_texts = processed_texts
 43
 44        # initialise dict with given models
 45        self.models = models
 46
 47        # initialise dict for cached indexes
 48        self._cache: dict[str, SearchEngine] = {}
 49
 50    def available_indexes(self) -> list[str]:
 51        """
 52        :returns: list of supported index names
 53        """
 54        return list(self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES)
 55
 56    def get_engine(self, index_name: str) -> SearchEngine:
 57        """
 58        Create SearchEngine instance if cached one doesn't exist
 59        :param index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext
 60        :returns: ready SearchEngine with loaded artifacts
 61        """
 62        # check if exists in cache
 63        key = index_name.lower().strip()
 64        if key in self._cache:
 65            return self._cache[key]
 66
 67        # check if indexer is supported
 68        if key not in self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES:
 69            raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')
 70
 71        # load indexer
 72        indexer = self._load_indexer(key)
 73
 74        # create engine
 75        engine = SearchEngine(indexer=indexer, preprocessor=self.preprocessor)
 76
 77        # set texts without rebuilding loaded index artifacts
 78        engine.original_texts = self.original_texts
 79        engine.processed_texts = self.processed_texts
 80
 81        self._cache[key] = engine
 82        return engine
 83
 84    def _load_indexer(self, index_name: str):
 85        """
 86        Internal function to make indexer ready
 87        :param index_name: target backend name
 88        :returns: loaded indexer instance
 89        """
 90        if index_name == "bm25":
 91            return BM25Indexer.load(self.indexes_root / "bm25")
 92
 93        if index_name == "word2vec":
 94            model = self.models.get("word2vec")
 95            if model is None:
 96                raise ValueError("word2vec model is not provided")
 97            return Word2VecIndexer.load(self.indexes_root / "word2vec", model=model)
 98
 99        if index_name == "fasttext":
100            model = self.models.get("fasttext")
101            if model is None:
102                raise ValueError("fasttext model is not provided")
103            return FastTextIndexer.load(self.indexes_root / "fasttext", model=model)
104
105        raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')
106
107    def clear_cache(self) -> None:
108        """
109        Clear cached engines
110        """
111        self._cache.clear()

Create and cache SearchEngine objects for different indexers

SearchEngineFactory( indexes_root: pathlib.Path, preprocessor, original_texts: list[str], processed_texts: list[str], models: dict) 
15    def __init__(
16            self,
17            indexes_root: Path,
18            preprocessor,
19            original_texts: list[str],
20            processed_texts: list[str],
21            models: dict,
22    ):
23        """
24        :param indexes_root: root directory with index artifacts
25        :param preprocessor: text preprocessor to preprocess queries
26        :param original_texts: original corpus texts for output
27        :param processed_texts: preprocessed corpus texts for searching
28        :param models: embedding models if needed, e.g. {"word2vec": w2v_model, "fasttext": ft_model}.
29        """
30        # check if preprocessed texts and original ones differ
31        if len(original_texts) != len(processed_texts):
32            raise ValueError("original_texts and processed_texts must have equal length")
33
34        # initialise main vars for indexers
35        self.indexes_root = Path(indexes_root)
36
37        # define preprocessor module
38        self.preprocessor = preprocessor
39
40        # initialise data
41        self.original_texts = original_texts
42        self.processed_texts = processed_texts
43
44        # initialise dict with given models
45        self.models = models
46
47        # initialise dict for cached indexes
48        self._cache: dict[str, SearchEngine] = {}
Parameters
  • indexes_root: root directory with index artifacts
  • preprocessor: text preprocessor to preprocess queries
  • original_texts: original corpus texts for output
  • processed_texts: preprocessed corpus texts for searching
  • models: embedding models if needed, e.g. {"word2vec": w2v_model, "fasttext": ft_model}.
SUPPORTED_INDEXES = ('bm25', 'word2vec', 'fasttext')
indexes_root
preprocessor
original_texts
processed_texts
models
def available_indexes(self) -> list[str]: 
50    def available_indexes(self) -> list[str]:
51        """
52        :returns: list of supported index names
53        """
54        return list(self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES)

:returns: list of supported index names

def get_engine(self, index_name: str) -> SearchEngine: 
56    def get_engine(self, index_name: str) -> SearchEngine:
57        """
58        Create SearchEngine instance if cached one doesn't exist
59        :param index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext
60        :returns: ready SearchEngine with loaded artifacts
61        """
62        # check if exists in cache
63        key = index_name.lower().strip()
64        if key in self._cache:
65            return self._cache[key]
66
67        # check if indexer is supported
68        if key not in self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES:
69            raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')
70
71        # load indexer
72        indexer = self._load_indexer(key)
73
74        # create engine
75        engine = SearchEngine(indexer=indexer, preprocessor=self.preprocessor)
76
77        # set texts without rebuilding loaded index artifacts
78        engine.original_texts = self.original_texts
79        engine.processed_texts = self.processed_texts
80
81        self._cache[key] = engine
82        return engine

Create SearchEngine instance if cached one doesn't exist

Parameters
  • index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext :returns: ready SearchEngine with loaded artifacts
def clear_cache(self) -> None: 
107    def clear_cache(self) -> None:
108        """
109        Clear cached engines
110        """
111        self._cache.clear()

Clear cached engines

class IndexBuildService: 
 22class IndexBuildService:
 23    """Service to check and build indexes and save them"""
 24
 25    SUPPORTED_INDEXES = ("bm25", "word2vec", "fasttext")
 26
 27    def __init__(
 28            self,
 29            corpus_csv_path: Path,
 30            indexes_root: Path,
 31            processed_text_column: str,
 32            models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 33    ) -> None:
 34        """
 35        :param corpus_csv_path: path to preprocessed corpus csv
 36        :param indexes_root: root dir where indexes will be stored
 37        :param processed_text_column: column name with preprocessed text (space tokens)
 38        :param models: embedding models dict, keys: word2vec`` and fasttext
 39        """
 40        self.corpus_csv_path = Path(corpus_csv_path)
 41        self.indexes_root = Path(indexes_root)
 42        self.processed_text_column = processed_text_column
 43        self.models = models or {}
 44
 45    def ensure_indexes(
 46            self,
 47            index_names: Optional[list[str]] = None,
 48            rebuild: bool = False,
 49    ) -> list[IndexBuildReport]:
 50        """
 51        Check indexes and build missing ones
 52        :param index_names: list of indexes to ensure (default: all supported)
 53        :param rebuild: if true, rebuild index even if artifacts exist
 54        :returns: list of reports
 55        """
 56        targets = index_names or list(self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES)
 57        reports: list[IndexBuildReport] = []
 58
 59        for name in targets:
 60            key = name.lower().strip()
 61            if key not in self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES:
 62                raise ValueError(f'index "{name}" is not supported')
 63
 64            index_dir = self.indexes_root / key
 65            existed = self.index_exists(key)
 66            built = False
 67
 68            if rebuild or not existed:
 69                self.build_index(key)
 70                built = True
 71
 72            reports.append(
 73                IndexBuildReport(
 74                    index_name=key,
 75                    existed=existed,
 76                    built=built,
 77                    index_dir=str(index_dir),
 78                )
 79            )
 80
 81        return reports
 82
 83    def index_exists(self, index_name: str) -> bool:
 84        """
 85        Check if index artifacts exist on disk
 86        :param index_name: one of bm25``, word2vec``, fasttext
 87        :returns: true if required artifacts exist
 88        """
 89        key = index_name.lower().strip()
 90        index_dir = self.indexes_root / key
 91
 92        meta_path = index_dir / "meta.json"
 93        if not meta_path.exists():
 94            return False
 95
 96        # check required files for index type
 97        if key == "bm25":
 98            return (index_dir / "bm25.pkl").exists()
 99
100        if key in ("word2vec", "fasttext"):
101            return (index_dir / "doc_vectors.npy").exists() and (index_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl").exists()
102
103        return False
104
105    def build_index(self, index_name: str) -> None:
106        """
107        Build a single index and save it to disk
108        :param index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext
109        :return: None
110        """
111        key = index_name.lower().strip()
112        if key not in self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES:
113            raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')
114
115        # create root dir if missing
116        self.indexes_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
117
118        # load corpus
119        if not self.corpus_csv_path.exists():
120            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing corpus csv: {self.corpus_csv_path}")
121
122        df = pd.read_csv(self.corpus_csv_path)
123        if self.processed_text_column not in df.columns:
124            raise ValueError(f'missing column "{self.processed_text_column}" in corpus')
125
126        processed_texts = df[self.processed_text_column].fillna("").astype(str).tolist()
127
128        # build and save selected indexer
129        index_dir = self.indexes_root / key
130
131        if key == "bm25":
132            indexer = BM25Indexer()
133            indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
134            indexer.save(index_dir)
135            return
136
137        if key == "word2vec":
138            model = self.models.get("word2vec")
139            if model is None:
140                raise ValueError("word2vec model is not provided")
141            indexer = Word2VecIndexer(model=model)
142            indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
143            indexer.save(index_dir)
144            return
145
146        if key == "fasttext":
147            model = self.models.get("fasttext")
148            if model is None:
149                raise ValueError("fasttext model is not provided")
150            indexer = FastTextIndexer(model=model)
151            indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
152            indexer.save(index_dir)
153            return
154
155        raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')
156
157    def read_index_meta(self, index_name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
158        """
159        Read meta.json for an index
160        :param index_name: index name
161        :returns: parsed meta dict
162        """
163        key = index_name.lower().strip()
164        meta_path = (self.indexes_root / key) / "meta.json"
165        if not meta_path.exists():
166            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {meta_path}")
167
168        with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
169            return json.load(f)

Service to check and build indexes and save them

IndexBuildService( corpus_csv_path: pathlib.Path, indexes_root: pathlib.Path, processed_text_column: str, models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None) 
27    def __init__(
28            self,
29            corpus_csv_path: Path,
30            indexes_root: Path,
31            processed_text_column: str,
32            models: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
33    ) -> None:
34        """
35        :param corpus_csv_path: path to preprocessed corpus csv
36        :param indexes_root: root dir where indexes will be stored
37        :param processed_text_column: column name with preprocessed text (space tokens)
38        :param models: embedding models dict, keys: word2vec`` and fasttext
39        """
40        self.corpus_csv_path = Path(corpus_csv_path)
41        self.indexes_root = Path(indexes_root)
42        self.processed_text_column = processed_text_column
43        self.models = models or {}
Parameters
  • corpus_csv_path: path to preprocessed corpus csv
  • indexes_root: root dir where indexes will be stored
  • processed_text_column: column name with preprocessed text (space tokens)
  • models: embedding models dict, keys: word2vec`` and fasttext
SUPPORTED_INDEXES = ('bm25', 'word2vec', 'fasttext')
corpus_csv_path
indexes_root
processed_text_column
models
def ensure_indexes( self, index_names: Optional[list[str]] = None, rebuild: bool = False) -> list[IndexBuildReport]: 
45    def ensure_indexes(
46            self,
47            index_names: Optional[list[str]] = None,
48            rebuild: bool = False,
49    ) -> list[IndexBuildReport]:
50        """
51        Check indexes and build missing ones
52        :param index_names: list of indexes to ensure (default: all supported)
53        :param rebuild: if true, rebuild index even if artifacts exist
54        :returns: list of reports
55        """
56        targets = index_names or list(self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES)
57        reports: list[IndexBuildReport] = []
58
59        for name in targets:
60            key = name.lower().strip()
61            if key not in self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES:
62                raise ValueError(f'index "{name}" is not supported')
63
64            index_dir = self.indexes_root / key
65            existed = self.index_exists(key)
66            built = False
67
68            if rebuild or not existed:
69                self.build_index(key)
70                built = True
71
72            reports.append(
73                IndexBuildReport(
74                    index_name=key,
75                    existed=existed,
76                    built=built,
77                    index_dir=str(index_dir),
78                )
79            )
80
81        return reports

Check indexes and build missing ones

Parameters
  • index_names: list of indexes to ensure (default: all supported)
  • rebuild: if true, rebuild index even if artifacts exist :returns: list of reports
def index_exists(self, index_name: str) -> bool: 
 83    def index_exists(self, index_name: str) -> bool:
 84        """
 85        Check if index artifacts exist on disk
 86        :param index_name: one of bm25``, word2vec``, fasttext
 87        :returns: true if required artifacts exist
 88        """
 89        key = index_name.lower().strip()
 90        index_dir = self.indexes_root / key
 91
 92        meta_path = index_dir / "meta.json"
 93        if not meta_path.exists():
 94            return False
 95
 96        # check required files for index type
 97        if key == "bm25":
 98            return (index_dir / "bm25.pkl").exists()
 99
100        if key in ("word2vec", "fasttext"):
101            return (index_dir / "doc_vectors.npy").exists() and (index_dir / "tfidf_vectorizer.pkl").exists()
102
103        return False

Check if index artifacts exist on disk

Parameters
  • index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext :returns: true if required artifacts exist
def build_index(self, index_name: str) -> None: 
105    def build_index(self, index_name: str) -> None:
106        """
107        Build a single index and save it to disk
108        :param index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext
109        :return: None
110        """
111        key = index_name.lower().strip()
112        if key not in self.SUPPORTED_INDEXES:
113            raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')
114
115        # create root dir if missing
116        self.indexes_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
117
118        # load corpus
119        if not self.corpus_csv_path.exists():
120            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing corpus csv: {self.corpus_csv_path}")
121
122        df = pd.read_csv(self.corpus_csv_path)
123        if self.processed_text_column not in df.columns:
124            raise ValueError(f'missing column "{self.processed_text_column}" in corpus')
125
126        processed_texts = df[self.processed_text_column].fillna("").astype(str).tolist()
127
128        # build and save selected indexer
129        index_dir = self.indexes_root / key
130
131        if key == "bm25":
132            indexer = BM25Indexer()
133            indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
134            indexer.save(index_dir)
135            return
136
137        if key == "word2vec":
138            model = self.models.get("word2vec")
139            if model is None:
140                raise ValueError("word2vec model is not provided")
141            indexer = Word2VecIndexer(model=model)
142            indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
143            indexer.save(index_dir)
144            return
145
146        if key == "fasttext":
147            model = self.models.get("fasttext")
148            if model is None:
149                raise ValueError("fasttext model is not provided")
150            indexer = FastTextIndexer(model=model)
151            indexer.build_index(processed_texts)
152            indexer.save(index_dir)
153            return
154
155        raise ValueError(f'index "{index_name}" is not supported')

Build a single index and save it to disk

Parameters
  • index_name: one of bm25, word2vec, fasttext
Returns

None

def read_index_meta(self, index_name: str) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
157    def read_index_meta(self, index_name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
158        """
159        Read meta.json for an index
160        :param index_name: index name
161        :returns: parsed meta dict
162        """
163        key = index_name.lower().strip()
164        meta_path = (self.indexes_root / key) / "meta.json"
165        if not meta_path.exists():
166            raise FileNotFoundError(f"missing file: {meta_path}")
167
168        with open(meta_path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
169            return json.load(f)

Read meta.json for an index

Parameters
  • index_name: index name :returns: parsed meta dict
@dataclass(frozen=True)
class IndexBuildReport: 
13@dataclass(frozen=True)
14class IndexBuildReport:
15    """Simple report for index building"""
16    index_name: str
17    existed: bool
18    built: bool
19    index_dir: str

Simple report for index building

IndexBuildReport(index_name: str, existed: bool, built: bool, index_dir: str)
index_name: str
existed: bool
built: bool
index_dir: str
class RecommendationService: 
 10class RecommendationService:
 11    """Service for collection recommendations with profile and studies (seed) fusion"""
 12
 13    def __init__(
 14        self,
 15        collection_manager: CollectionManager,
 16        search_service: SearchService,
 17        docs_df: pd.DataFrame,
 18        default_index_name: str = "fasttext",
 19        seed_text_column: str = "index_text_lemmatised",
 20        rrf_k: int = 60,
 21    ) -> None:
 22        """
 23        :param collection_manager: manager with collections state
 24        :param search_service: search service with metadata enrichment
 25        :param docs_df: dataframe with corpus metadata
 26        :param default_index_name: default semantic index for recommendation
 27        :param seed_text_column: target text column for per-seed queries
 28        :param rrf_k: RRF denominator constant
 29        """
 30        self.collection_manager = collection_manager
 31        self.search_service = search_service
 32        self.docs_df = docs_df.reset_index(drop=True)
 33        self.default_index_name = default_index_name
 34        self.seed_text_column = seed_text_column
 35        self.rrf_k = rrf_k
 36
 37        # map paper id -> dataframe row for fast access
 38        self._docs_by_paper_id = self.docs_df.set_index("id", drop=False) if "id" in self.docs_df.columns else None
 39
 40    def recommend(
 41        self,
 42        collection_id: str,
 43        top_k: int = 10,
 44        filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 45        profile_candidates: int = 100,
 46        seed_candidates: int = 30,
 47        max_seeds: int = 10,
 48    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 49        """
 50        Main function to build recommendations for user's collection
 51        :param collection_id: target collection id
 52        :param top_k: number of recommendations
 53        :param filters: optional search filters
 54        :param profile_candidates: candidate size for profile-based search
 55        :param seed_candidates: candidate size for each research (seed) search
 56        :param max_seeds: limit number of seeds to search
 57        :returns: recommendation response
 58        """
 59        # recommendation pipeline:
 60        # 1 profile-based search by collection text (title+description+keywords)
 61        # 2 research search (studies as seeds) for each added paper
 62        # 3 RRF fusion:
 63        # internal seed RRF across seed lists
 64        # final RRF between profile list and fused seed list
 65
 66        start = perf_counter()
 67        collection = self.collection_manager.get_collection(collection_id)
 68        if collection is None:
 69            raise KeyError(f'collection "{collection_id}" not found')
 70
 71        # get stats from collection
 72        excluded_ids = set(collection.added_papers)
 73        profile_text = self.collection_manager.get_profile_text(collection_id)
 74
 75        # profile-based ranking
 76        profile_ranking: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 77        if profile_text:
 78            profile_response = self.search_service.search_with_filters(
 79                query=profile_text,
 80                index_name=self.default_index_name,
 81                top_k=profile_candidates,
 82                filters=filters,
 83            )
 84            profile_ranking = profile_response["results"]
 85
 86        # seed-based ranking (study-based search + internal seed fusion)
 87        seed_ids = list(collection.added_papers)[:max_seeds]
 88        # now we need to get lists of ranks for RRF
 89        # shape: list[list[dict[str, ...]]]
 90        seed_rankings = []
 91        for seed_id in seed_ids:
 92            seed_query = self._build_seed_query(seed_id)
 93            if not seed_query:
 94                continue
 95            seed_response = self.search_service.search_with_filters(
 96                query=seed_query,
 97                index_name=self.default_index_name,
 98                top_k=seed_candidates,
 99                filters=filters,
100            )
101            if seed_response["results"]:
102                seed_rankings.append(seed_response["results"])
103
104        fused_seed_ranking = self._fuse_rrf(seed_rankings, self.rrf_k)
105
106        # final fusion between profile list and seed list
107        # the same type as seed_rankings
108        final_rankings = []
109        if profile_ranking:
110            final_rankings.append(profile_ranking)
111        if fused_seed_ranking:
112            final_rankings.append(fused_seed_ranking)
113
114        fused_final = self._fuse_rrf(final_rankings, self.rrf_k)
115
116        # remove added papers and trim
117        recommendations = []
118        for item in fused_final:
119            paper_id = item["paper_id"]
120            if not paper_id or paper_id in excluded_ids:
121                continue
122            recommendations.append(item)
123            if len(recommendations) >= top_k:
124                break
125
126        elapsed_ms = (perf_counter() - start) * 1000.0
127
128        # add metatag for information about type of recommendation
129        mode = "warm_start" if collection.added_papers else "cold_start"
130
131        return {
132            "collection_id": collection.id,
133            "mode": mode,
134            "index_name": self.default_index_name,
135            "top_k": top_k,
136            "results_count": len(recommendations),
137            "elapsed_ms": round(elapsed_ms, 3),
138            "results": recommendations,
139        }
140
141    def _build_seed_query(self, paper_id: str) -> str:
142        """
143        Build seed query text from paper metadata
144        :param paper_id: corpus paper id
145        :returns: query string
146        """
147        if self._docs_by_paper_id is None:
148            return ""
149        if paper_id not in self._docs_by_paper_id.index:
150            return ""
151
152        row = self._docs_by_paper_id.loc[paper_id]
153
154        # prefer preprocessed text to match index representation
155        if self.seed_text_column in row.index:
156            prepared_text = str(row[self.seed_text_column] or "").strip()
157            if prepared_text:
158                return prepared_text
159
160        # fallback to raw text if prepared text is missing
161        title = str(row["title"] or "").strip() if "title" in row.index else ""
162        abstract = str(row["abstract"] or "").strip() if "abstract" in row.index else ""
163        return " ".join([p for p in [title, abstract] if p]).strip()
164
165    @staticmethod
166    def _fuse_rrf(rankings: list[list[dict[str, Any]]], rrf_k: int) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
167        """
168        Fuse many rankings using RRF
169        :param rankings: list of rankings (each ranking is list of hit dicts from search)
170        :param rrf_k: denominator constant in RRF formula
171        :returns: fused list sorted by recommendation_score
172        """
173        score_map: dict[str, float] = {}
174        best_item_map: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
175
176        for ranking in rankings:
177            for rank, item in enumerate(ranking, start=1):
178                paper_id = item.get("paper_id")
179                if not paper_id:
180                    continue
181
182                # RRF score contribution: 1 / (k + rank)
183                score_map[paper_id] = score_map.get(paper_id, 0.0) + (1.0 / float(rrf_k + rank))
184
185                # keep first occurrence as template
186                if paper_id not in best_item_map:
187                    best_item_map[paper_id] = dict(item)
188
189        fused = []
190        for paper_id, score in score_map.items():
191            payload = dict(best_item_map[paper_id])
192            payload["recommendation_score"] = round(float(score), 8)
193            fused.append(payload)
194
195        fused.sort(key=lambda x: x["recommendation_score"], reverse=True)
196        return fused

Service for collection recommendations with profile and studies (seed) fusion

RecommendationService( collection_manager: CollectionManager, search_service: SearchService, docs_df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, default_index_name: str = 'fasttext', seed_text_column: str = 'index_text_lemmatised', rrf_k: int = 60) 
13    def __init__(
14        self,
15        collection_manager: CollectionManager,
16        search_service: SearchService,
17        docs_df: pd.DataFrame,
18        default_index_name: str = "fasttext",
19        seed_text_column: str = "index_text_lemmatised",
20        rrf_k: int = 60,
21    ) -> None:
22        """
23        :param collection_manager: manager with collections state
24        :param search_service: search service with metadata enrichment
25        :param docs_df: dataframe with corpus metadata
26        :param default_index_name: default semantic index for recommendation
27        :param seed_text_column: target text column for per-seed queries
28        :param rrf_k: RRF denominator constant
29        """
30        self.collection_manager = collection_manager
31        self.search_service = search_service
32        self.docs_df = docs_df.reset_index(drop=True)
33        self.default_index_name = default_index_name
34        self.seed_text_column = seed_text_column
35        self.rrf_k = rrf_k
36
37        # map paper id -> dataframe row for fast access
38        self._docs_by_paper_id = self.docs_df.set_index("id", drop=False) if "id" in self.docs_df.columns else None
Parameters
  • collection_manager: manager with collections state
  • search_service: search service with metadata enrichment
  • docs_df: dataframe with corpus metadata
  • default_index_name: default semantic index for recommendation
  • seed_text_column: target text column for per-seed queries
  • rrf_k: RRF denominator constant
collection_manager
search_service
docs_df
default_index_name
seed_text_column
rrf_k
def recommend( self, collection_id: str, top_k: int = 10, filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None, profile_candidates: int = 100, seed_candidates: int = 30, max_seeds: int = 10) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
 40    def recommend(
 41        self,
 42        collection_id: str,
 43        top_k: int = 10,
 44        filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 45        profile_candidates: int = 100,
 46        seed_candidates: int = 30,
 47        max_seeds: int = 10,
 48    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 49        """
 50        Main function to build recommendations for user's collection
 51        :param collection_id: target collection id
 52        :param top_k: number of recommendations
 53        :param filters: optional search filters
 54        :param profile_candidates: candidate size for profile-based search
 55        :param seed_candidates: candidate size for each research (seed) search
 56        :param max_seeds: limit number of seeds to search
 57        :returns: recommendation response
 58        """
 59        # recommendation pipeline:
 60        # 1 profile-based search by collection text (title+description+keywords)
 61        # 2 research search (studies as seeds) for each added paper
 62        # 3 RRF fusion:
 63        # internal seed RRF across seed lists
 64        # final RRF between profile list and fused seed list
 65
 66        start = perf_counter()
 67        collection = self.collection_manager.get_collection(collection_id)
 68        if collection is None:
 69            raise KeyError(f'collection "{collection_id}" not found')
 70
 71        # get stats from collection
 72        excluded_ids = set(collection.added_papers)
 73        profile_text = self.collection_manager.get_profile_text(collection_id)
 74
 75        # profile-based ranking
 76        profile_ranking: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
 77        if profile_text:
 78            profile_response = self.search_service.search_with_filters(
 79                query=profile_text,
 80                index_name=self.default_index_name,
 81                top_k=profile_candidates,
 82                filters=filters,
 83            )
 84            profile_ranking = profile_response["results"]
 85
 86        # seed-based ranking (study-based search + internal seed fusion)
 87        seed_ids = list(collection.added_papers)[:max_seeds]
 88        # now we need to get lists of ranks for RRF
 89        # shape: list[list[dict[str, ...]]]
 90        seed_rankings = []
 91        for seed_id in seed_ids:
 92            seed_query = self._build_seed_query(seed_id)
 93            if not seed_query:
 94                continue
 95            seed_response = self.search_service.search_with_filters(
 96                query=seed_query,
 97                index_name=self.default_index_name,
 98                top_k=seed_candidates,
 99                filters=filters,
100            )
101            if seed_response["results"]:
102                seed_rankings.append(seed_response["results"])
103
104        fused_seed_ranking = self._fuse_rrf(seed_rankings, self.rrf_k)
105
106        # final fusion between profile list and seed list
107        # the same type as seed_rankings
108        final_rankings = []
109        if profile_ranking:
110            final_rankings.append(profile_ranking)
111        if fused_seed_ranking:
112            final_rankings.append(fused_seed_ranking)
113
114        fused_final = self._fuse_rrf(final_rankings, self.rrf_k)
115
116        # remove added papers and trim
117        recommendations = []
118        for item in fused_final:
119            paper_id = item["paper_id"]
120            if not paper_id or paper_id in excluded_ids:
121                continue
122            recommendations.append(item)
123            if len(recommendations) >= top_k:
124                break
125
126        elapsed_ms = (perf_counter() - start) * 1000.0
127
128        # add metatag for information about type of recommendation
129        mode = "warm_start" if collection.added_papers else "cold_start"
130
131        return {
132            "collection_id": collection.id,
133            "mode": mode,
134            "index_name": self.default_index_name,
135            "top_k": top_k,
136            "results_count": len(recommendations),
137            "elapsed_ms": round(elapsed_ms, 3),
138            "results": recommendations,
139        }

Main function to build recommendations for user's collection

Parameters
  • collection_id: target collection id
  • top_k: number of recommendations
  • filters: optional search filters
  • profile_candidates: candidate size for profile-based search
  • seed_candidates: candidate size for each research (seed) search
  • max_seeds: limit number of seeds to search :returns: recommendation response
class SearchService: 
  9class SearchService:
 10    """Service to run search and enrich results with metadata and do filtered search"""
 11
 12    DEFAULT_METADATA_COLUMNS = (
 13        "id",
 14        "doi",
 15        "author",
 16        "title",
 17        "publication_year",
 18        "journal",
 19        "is_retracted",
 20        "cited_by_count",
 21        "field",
 22        "source",
 23    )
 24
 25    def __init__(
 26            self,
 27            engine_factory: SearchEngineFactory,
 28            docs_df: pd.DataFrame,
 29            metadata_columns: Optional[list[str]] = None,
 30            original_text_column: str = "abstract",
 31            processed_text_column: str = "index_text_lemmatised",
 32    ) -> None:
 33        """
 34        :param engine_factory: factory with ready indexers
 35        :param docs_df: dataframe with metadata and text columns
 36        :param metadata_columns: metadata columns to return in each hit
 37        :param original_text_column: column with original text
 38        :param processed_text_column: column with preprocessed text
 39        """
 40        self.engine_factory = engine_factory
 41        self.docs_df = docs_df.reset_index(drop=True)
 42        self.original_text_column = original_text_column
 43        self.processed_text_column = processed_text_column
 44
 45        if metadata_columns is None:
 46            metadata_columns = [c for c in self.DEFAULT_METADATA_COLUMNS if c in self.docs_df.columns]
 47        self.metadata_columns = metadata_columns
 48
 49        self._validate_dataframe_columns()
 50
 51    def _validate_dataframe_columns(self) -> None:
 52        """
 53        Check required columns in dataframe
 54        """
 55        required_columns = [self.original_text_column, self.processed_text_column]
 56        missing = [c for c in required_columns if c not in self.docs_df.columns]
 57        if missing:
 58            raise ValueError(f"missing required columns in docs_df: {missing}")
 59
 60    def search(self, query: str, index_name: str, top_k: int = 10) -> dict[str, Any]:
 61        """
 62        Run search and enrich hits with dataframe metadata
 63        :param query: query string
 64        :param index_name: target indexer name
 65        :param top_k: number of docs to return
 66        :returns: response with search metadata and hits
 67        """
 68        return self.search_with_filters(
 69            query=query,
 70            index_name=index_name,
 71            top_k=top_k,
 72            filters=None,
 73        )
 74
 75    def search_with_filters(
 76            self,
 77            query: str,
 78            index_name: str,
 79            top_k: int = 10,
 80            filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 81            oversample_factor: int = 5,
 82    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 83        """
 84        Run search, enrich hits with metadata and apply filters
 85        Supported filter keys:
 86        - is_retracted
 87        - publication_year_from
 88        - publication_year_to
 89        - journal_contains
 90        - field_in
 91        - cited_by_count_min
 92        - cited_by_count_max
 93        :param query: query string
 94        :param index_name: target indexer name
 95        :param top_k: number of docs to return
 96        :param filters: optional metadata filters
 97        :param oversample_factor: multiplier for candidate retrieval before filtering
 98        :returns: response with search metadata and hits
 99        """
100        start = perf_counter()
101
102        engine = self.engine_factory.get_engine(index_name=index_name)
103
104        # get more candidates if filters are enabled
105        candidate_k = top_k
106        if filters:
107            max_docs = len(self.docs_df)
108            candidate_k = min(max_docs, max(top_k, top_k * max(1, oversample_factor)))
109
110        raw_hits = engine.search(query=query, top_k=candidate_k)
111
112        elapsed_ms = (perf_counter() - start) * 1000.0
113
114        hits = []
115        for hit in raw_hits:
116            enriched = self._enrich_hit(hit)
117            if self._passes_filters(enriched, filters):
118                hits.append(enriched)
119            if len(hits) >= top_k:
120                break
121
122        return {
123            "query": query,
124            "index_name": index_name,
125            "top_k": top_k,
126            "filters": filters or {},
127            "results_count": len(hits),
128            "elapsed_ms": round(elapsed_ms, 3),
129            "results": hits,
130        }
131
132    def _enrich_hit(self, hit: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
133        """
134        Enrich one search hit with metadata from docs df
135        :param hit: raw hit from SearchEngine
136        :returns: enriched hit dict
137        """
138        doc_id = int(hit["id"])
139        row = self.docs_df.iloc[doc_id]
140        paper_id = row["id"] if "id" in row.index else None
141
142        enriched: dict[str, Any] = {
143            "paper_id": None if pd.isna(paper_id) else str(paper_id),
144            "doc_id": doc_id,
145            "score": hit["score"],
146            "text": hit["text"],
147            "preprocessed_text": hit["preprocessed_text"],
148        }
149
150        for column in self.metadata_columns:
151            value = row[column] if column in row.index else None
152            if pd.isna(value):
153                value = None
154            enriched[column] = value
155
156        return enriched
157
158    @staticmethod
159    def _passes_filters(hit: dict[str, Any], filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]]) -> bool:
160        """
161        Check if enriched hit passes metadata filters
162        :param hit: enriched hit dict
163        :param filters: filters dict
164        :returns: true if hit should be kept
165        """
166        if not filters:
167            return True
168
169        # filter by retraction flag
170        if "is_retracted" in filters:
171            expected = bool(filters["is_retracted"])
172            actual = bool(hit["is_retracted"]) if hit["is_retracted"] is not None else False
173            if actual != expected:
174                return False
175
176        # filter by publication year interval
177        year = hit["publication_year"]
178        if "publication_year_from" in filters and year is not None:
179            if int(year) < int(filters["publication_year_from"]):
180                return False
181        if "publication_year_to" in filters and year is not None:
182            if int(year) > int(filters["publication_year_to"]):
183                return False
184
185        # filter by journal substring
186        if "journal_contains" in filters:
187            needle = str(filters["journal_contains"]).strip().lower()
188            journal = str(hit["journal"] or "").lower()
189            if needle and needle not in journal:
190                return False
191
192        # filter by field inclusion
193        if "field_in" in filters:
194            allowed = {str(v).strip().lower() for v in filters["field_in"] if str(v).strip()}
195            field = str(hit["field"] or "").strip().lower()
196            if allowed and field not in allowed:
197                return False
198
199        # filter by cited count interval
200        cited = hit["cited_by_count"]
201        if cited is not None:
202            if "cited_by_count_min" in filters and int(cited) < int(filters["cited_by_count_min"]):
203                return False
204            if "cited_by_count_max" in filters and int(cited) > int(filters["cited_by_count_max"]):
205                return False
206
207        return True

Service to run search and enrich results with metadata and do filtered search

SearchService( engine_factory: SearchEngineFactory, docs_df: pandas.core.frame.DataFrame, metadata_columns: Optional[list[str]] = None, original_text_column: str = 'abstract', processed_text_column: str = 'index_text_lemmatised') 
25    def __init__(
26            self,
27            engine_factory: SearchEngineFactory,
28            docs_df: pd.DataFrame,
29            metadata_columns: Optional[list[str]] = None,
30            original_text_column: str = "abstract",
31            processed_text_column: str = "index_text_lemmatised",
32    ) -> None:
33        """
34        :param engine_factory: factory with ready indexers
35        :param docs_df: dataframe with metadata and text columns
36        :param metadata_columns: metadata columns to return in each hit
37        :param original_text_column: column with original text
38        :param processed_text_column: column with preprocessed text
39        """
40        self.engine_factory = engine_factory
41        self.docs_df = docs_df.reset_index(drop=True)
42        self.original_text_column = original_text_column
43        self.processed_text_column = processed_text_column
44
45        if metadata_columns is None:
46            metadata_columns = [c for c in self.DEFAULT_METADATA_COLUMNS if c in self.docs_df.columns]
47        self.metadata_columns = metadata_columns
48
49        self._validate_dataframe_columns()
Parameters
  • engine_factory: factory with ready indexers
  • docs_df: dataframe with metadata and text columns
  • metadata_columns: metadata columns to return in each hit
  • original_text_column: column with original text
  • processed_text_column: column with preprocessed text
DEFAULT_METADATA_COLUMNS = ('id', 'doi', 'author', 'title', 'publication_year', 'journal', 'is_retracted', 'cited_by_count', 'field', 'source')
engine_factory
docs_df
original_text_column
processed_text_column
metadata_columns
def search( self, query: str, index_name: str, top_k: int = 10) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
60    def search(self, query: str, index_name: str, top_k: int = 10) -> dict[str, Any]:
61        """
62        Run search and enrich hits with dataframe metadata
63        :param query: query string
64        :param index_name: target indexer name
65        :param top_k: number of docs to return
66        :returns: response with search metadata and hits
67        """
68        return self.search_with_filters(
69            query=query,
70            index_name=index_name,
71            top_k=top_k,
72            filters=None,
73        )

Run search and enrich hits with dataframe metadata

Parameters
  • query: query string
  • index_name: target indexer name
  • top_k: number of docs to return :returns: response with search metadata and hits
def search_with_filters( self, query: str, index_name: str, top_k: int = 10, filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None, oversample_factor: int = 5) -> dict[str, typing.Any]: 
 75    def search_with_filters(
 76            self,
 77            query: str,
 78            index_name: str,
 79            top_k: int = 10,
 80            filters: Optional[dict[str, Any]] = None,
 81            oversample_factor: int = 5,
 82    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
 83        """
 84        Run search, enrich hits with metadata and apply filters
 85        Supported filter keys:
 86        - is_retracted
 87        - publication_year_from
 88        - publication_year_to
 89        - journal_contains
 90        - field_in
 91        - cited_by_count_min
 92        - cited_by_count_max
 93        :param query: query string
 94        :param index_name: target indexer name
 95        :param top_k: number of docs to return
 96        :param filters: optional metadata filters
 97        :param oversample_factor: multiplier for candidate retrieval before filtering
 98        :returns: response with search metadata and hits
 99        """
100        start = perf_counter()
101
102        engine = self.engine_factory.get_engine(index_name=index_name)
103
104        # get more candidates if filters are enabled
105        candidate_k = top_k
106        if filters:
107            max_docs = len(self.docs_df)
108            candidate_k = min(max_docs, max(top_k, top_k * max(1, oversample_factor)))
109
110        raw_hits = engine.search(query=query, top_k=candidate_k)
111
112        elapsed_ms = (perf_counter() - start) * 1000.0
113
114        hits = []
115        for hit in raw_hits:
116            enriched = self._enrich_hit(hit)
117            if self._passes_filters(enriched, filters):
118                hits.append(enriched)
119            if len(hits) >= top_k:
120                break
121
122        return {
123            "query": query,
124            "index_name": index_name,
125            "top_k": top_k,
126            "filters": filters or {},
127            "results_count": len(hits),
128            "elapsed_ms": round(elapsed_ms, 3),
129            "results": hits,
130        }

Run search, enrich hits with metadata and apply filters Supported filter keys:

  • is_retracted
  • publication_year_from
  • publication_year_to
  • journal_contains
  • field_in
  • cited_by_count_min
  • cited_by_count_max
Parameters
  • query: query string
  • index_name: target indexer name
  • top_k: number of docs to return
  • filters: optional metadata filters
  • oversample_factor: multiplier for candidate retrieval before filtering :returns: response with search metadata and hits
